title: Director, Data Analytics & Insights
description:
Come Work with Us!

At RBC, our culture is deeply supportive and rich in opportunity and reward. You will help our clients thrive and our communities prosper, empowered by a spirit of shared purpose.

Whether you’re helping clients find new opportunities, developing new technology, or providing expert advice to internal partners, you will be doing work that matters in the world, in an environment built on teamwork, service, responsibility, diversity, and integrity.

Job Title

Director, Data Analytics & Insights

Job Description

What is the opportunity?

The PSI (Personal Savings & Investments) Strategy & Analytics team collaborates with all Mutual Fund Distribution, GIC & Savings and Direct Investing product owners to support the creation and evolution of cross PSI LOB strategies.

In this role, you are responsible to provide leadership in the development and implementation of PSI’s Data and Analytics Strategy and associated capabilities to transform the experience for PSI clients through the Investments MegaJourney. The aim of the initiatives under this strategy is to provide leadership and stand-up a data squad that enhances the client experience from front-to-back and end-to-end; leverage data to drive initiatives to improve client engagement; and produce impactful analytics to senior leadership and drive business strategy. You have the opportunity to work cross-functionally within RBC (data, digital, marketing, operations, regulatory) to ensure alignment in data and analytics to support the MegaJourney. Partner with internal and external partners to identify partnership opportunities that are beneficial to PSI/RBC and maximize shareholder value by optimizing the client experience, driving profitability and efficiency through business insights supported by best-in-class data and analytics.

What will you do?

Drive PSI critical business imperatives to unlock growth and develop client’s relationship with RBC

  • Provide thought leadership and serve as a centre of expertise on the use of data as a strategic asset for RBC, particularly within the PSI businesses
  • Offer business and strategic leadership to an agile data team of project personnel and stakeholders in the development and implementation of a series of initiatives to transform the front-to-back and end-to-end processes for PSI clients
  • Inform strategy for a robust data infrastructure providing accurate and consistent analytics and reporting within PSI, and drive initiatives to augment the EDW / data lake environments with new data and analytics capabilities with objective of delivering a more holistic retail investing and banking client experience
  • Transform the way PSI uses data by proactively identifying opportunities, implementing advanced reporting and analytics tools and processes to enable us to be a data and insights-driven business
  • Responsible for innovation and taking ideas, relating to the PSI business, from concept to implementation
  • Expected to influence executives and other key decision makers by leveraging their experience, logical approach to problem solving as well as his/her excellent ability to present and communicate
  • Leads the market with competitive insights to support the deep client understanding required to underpin competitively differentiated experiences
  • Consistently deliver data driven insights and analytics to drive business and strategic decisions
  • Establish data strategy and infrastructure with key partners to support Investments MegaJourney and drive future analytics

What do you need to succeed?

Behavioral Competencies:

  • Improves Performance: Sets and pursues a standard for performance which is higher than that set by management. Measures outcomes against that standard. Makes specific changes in the system or in own work methods to improve performance (e.g., efficiency/effectiveness, improves quality, customer/service partner satisfaction, profitable revenue growth.
  • Strong customer service attitude: Seeks innovative means of satisfying client needs in a digital channel while maintaining profitability in a well-controlled environment.
  • Adapts to the situation: Acts decisively based on the circumstances of the situation. Enjoys the challenge of unfamiliar assignments.
  • Builds trust and Fosters Win-Win Mentality: Tailors approach, presentations or discussions to appeal to the interest and level of others.
  • Identifies Opportunities for Business Improvement: Questions and challenges existing processes, procedures, methodologies and/or technologies. Investigates or creates new ways to look at current practices to develop new solutions to existing problems or practices. Recognizes a current technology or business opportunity and finds ways to leverage the current capabilities of RBC Financial Group to take advantage of these opportunities.
  • Provides Rationale: Understands basic business issues. Goes beyond answering the question. Helps others resolve basic problems related to own area of expertise. Provides information people need to know to do their job and make accurate decisions.
  • Execution Excellence: Drives performance and sustains/drives results from self and others through professional excellence and effective execution. Manages high-performance teams and holds them accountable for delivering results. Prepares for implementation by identifying the interdependencies throughout the process and by taking the necessary action to escalate potential issues.
  • Develops Self and Others: Focuses on self-development, coaching, mentoring and development of others. Fosters an environment where great work is encouraged and celebrated.
  • Data mining skills using SAS, SQL, R, or Python; solid technical skill set including the creation and delivery of customer analytics, research and profitability results into business outcomes

Key Technical Competencies

  • Strong analytical skills demonstrated by leveraging data to drive insights and value to an organization
  • Strategic mindset and vision of the future for the PSI businesses
  • Well-developed leadership and relationship competencies (ability to lead internal, cross-enterprise, and external teams)
  • Strong business acumen and ability to conceptualize changes in market conditions and leverage innovative thinking to develop and capitalize on strategic opportunities
  • Strong impact & influence
  • Expert knowledge of working with data in relational databases and/or Hadoop
  • Proven technical skills and experience at using analytical software tools and applications such as SAS, Tableau, SQL, Python

What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.

  • Comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock where applicable
  • Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
  • Ability to make a difference and lasting impact
  • Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
  • Flexible work/life balance options
  • Opportunities to do challenging work
  • Opportunities to take on progressively greater accountabilities
  • Access to a variety of job opportunities across business and geographies

TORONTO, Ontario, Canada

title: Manager, Forecasting Models - Retail & Small Business (Contract)
description:
Requisition ID: 156183

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

Overview


The world is changing faster than ever and so are we!

“Big data”, “machine learning” and “AI” are not just buzzwords; they are an ever-increasing part of our daily life. We have decades of data powering hundreds of models deployed across a multitude of systems that are crying out for smart talented team players who are results-oriented, embrace transformational challenges and relish the opportunity to help us turn our vision into reality.

Our goal is to be nothing less than The Leading Bank in The Americas by putting Customers First. Achieving this requires a Winning Team that can build, manage and govern stable high-performing models and provide our business partners with the best analytic insights and advice possible. To put things in perspective, the models that we build underpin automated risk-management decisions and forecasts that impact millions of customers and keep hundreds of $billions safe every single day.

What Are We Looking For?


We want talented pragmatic analytically oriented data scientists who are equally at home interpreting and applying statistics as they are ingesting and massaging data, translating regulatory requirements into models and explaining complex issues in simple concise ways. We want bright and energetic minds who come armed with a bottomless sense of curiosity about everything (great models and analytics are about so much more than just the numbers!), a passion for getting things done right – the first time – and a deep sense of personal and professional accountability.

To be a Manager in Forecasting arm of Retail Models & Analytics, it isn’t enough to just be a great model developer (though that is incredibly important as well); you also need to be a collaborative team player who actively listens to stakeholders and customers because you are passionate about helping them win and you need to be interested in building something lasting and great. Working with internal customers (stakeholders) across the bank to understand and translate their needs into outcomes, exploring both novel and legacy data sets and methodologies, collaborating with technology partners to get things implemented flawlessly, and monitoring, tuning up (when necessary), and governing the models that your team “owns” will all be equally important.

We are results driven, we work hard, we play hard, we nerd out and we bring service, smiles and solutions to every problem that we face; so should you.


Make an Impact


As a Manager, you will report directly to a Senior Manager or Director and be a critical member of a team overseeing a portfolio of forecasting models and analytic processes. You will support – from conception through execution and governance – the provision (credit loss estimation) models covering all of the Bank’s retail and small business product lines. You will collaborate, on a regular basis, with a wide range of stakeholders and internal partners including Provisioning, Model Operations, Enterprise Stress Testing, Product, Model Validation and Governance, Technology & Data, Finance, Compliance and Audit.

You will have access to both traditional SAS tools and a modern machine learning stack that includes on-premise GPU computing, open source Jupyter development environments, a Hadoop cluster, containerized API model deployment and data visualization business intelligence tools.

Under the guidance of your Director, your team of risk modeling experts will use these tools to develop advanced risk prediction and loss estimation forecasting models that will be used to make decisions worth $billions every month and therefore they need to be not only precise and accurate, but highly stable, explainable, compliant, secure and useful. You will be responsible for understanding the goals & priorities set for you, executing them efficiently with a perpetual eye on quality, asking questions often and delivering results in harmony with your teammates.

Is This Role Right for You?

  • You believe, first and foremost, in always doing the right thing and doing things the right way
  • You are both deeply curious and highly pragmatic
  • You are comfortable with ambitious but realistic goals and are committed to achieving them
  • You are social, engaging, not afraid to ask questions and relish the opportunity to learn from global partners across the firm (who may be less technical than you and might not speak English)
  • You relish the opportunity and yet also understand the responsibility and weight that comes with the fact that the output of your models will directly influence the bank’s financial statements
  • You can read complex and lengthy regulatory requirements and identify opportunities for optimization while always remaining committed to compliance excellence
  • You understand what PDs, LGDs, EADS & attrition really are, from the ground up, and can model them precisely for any product
  • You have a genuine love, passion and talent for programming, data, and predictive modeling
  • You are committed to quality in everything you do – statistically, process and governance wise
  • You look forward to sharing your insights through high-quality verbal & written communications
  • You are professional, motivated AND FUN. We mean it.

Sample projects that you might work on include:

  • Complete specialized analysis, develop statistical solutions and recommend improvements to estimation techniques and/or forecasting models
  • Prepare attribution and/or stock-flow analysis of product portfolios
  • Collaborate with risk management, vendors, technology, foreign counterparts and compliance + audit to ensure effective governance, measurement & communication of model performance
  • Conduct research of new innovative modeling methodologies, tools and regulatory requirements
  • Lead the periodic monitoring, recalibration and maintenance of models and analytic tools
  • Identify, promote and lead process improvements for model development and monitoring
  • Ensure that the data used for model development is reliable and robust and that it adheres to industry and regulatory standards

What Do You Need to Succeed?


General

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering or an equivalent technical field
  • A demonstrated history of analytic problem solving and sound judgment
  • Experience as an analyst or data scientist with a track record of accomplishments working on cross-functional teams
  • Strong English-language verbal and written communications skills: the ability to distill abstract business requirements into analysis, concisely convey technical outcomes, tell a story visually with data, prepare and edit high-quality documentation and be accepted as a trusted advisor by peers
  • Able to work remotely and on-site on multiple activities simultaneously and meet deadlines

Technical

  • Strong fundamental understanding of statistics
  • 2+ years of overall experience in analytic roles developing and using predictive, risk and/or forecasting models
  • Highly proficient in 1 or more analytical programming languages (Python, R, SAS, etc.), SQL (Teradata, Oracle, DB2, SQL Server) and multiple environments (Unix & Windows)
  • 1 or more years of experience with big data platforms / integration (Hadoop, Spark, Hive, Avro)
  • Knowledge about credit risk models / scorecards

Nice to Have’s

  • Proficient reading, writing and speaking Spanish
  • Experience with credit risk forecasting and/or financial reporting frameworks
  • Experience developing credit risk models / scorecards
  • Experience working within loss forecasting, provisioning, IFRS 9 or CECL frameworks
  • Domain expertise with any retail & small business banking products and/or risk management practices
  • FRM, CFA credentials
  • Experience training and deploying machine learning models using common Python open source frameworks (i.e., scikit-learn) and/or Python DevOps
  • Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Teams, PowerBI) power user
  • Experience working with FICO / TSYS / CGI decision systems (TRIAD, OM, ACE, CACS)

What’s in It for You?

  • We have an inclusive and collaborative work environment that values curiosity & ownership, encourages pragmatic creativity (i.e. true innovation), and celebrates success!
  • You'll get to work with and learn from an incredibly friendly and diverse group of accomplished leaders
  • Access to thousands of online and in person courses so you can brush up on skills, or learn new ones
  • Multiple career paths and innumerable progression opportunities. We hire you for your talent and not just for the job. We want to see you succeed not just in your role but in your career as a whole
  • A competitive rewards package that includes a base salary, a performance bonus, company matching programs on pension and profit sharing, paid vacation, personal & sick days, medical, vision and dental benefits that start from day one and much more!

Location(s): Canada : Ontario : Toronto

Scotiabank is a leading bank in the Americas. Guided by our purpose: "for every future", we help our customers, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets.

At Scotiabank, we value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. If you require accommodation (including, but not limited to, an accessible interview site, alternate format documents, ASL Interpreter, or Assistive Technology) during the recruitment and selection process, please let our Recruitment team know. We thank all applicants for their interest in a career at Scotiabank; however, only those candidates who are selected for an interview will be contacted.

title: Data Analyst - Secondment Position
description:
What’s the opportunity?

Borealis AI is looking for an expert Data Analyst Lead to own the data domain for our Credit portfolio to drive our development of state of the art machine learning models. In this role, the Data Analyst Lead will work closely with our partner teams at RBC to explore and discover various data sources that might be of interest to our research, analyze the data to understand its properties and semantics, work alongside our researchers to provide the datasets needed for ML model development, and collaborate with our engineering team to develop data tools and automated pipelines in productionization of the models. The successful candidate will use their talent and expertise to own the data domain and make if available with all parties involved through effective communication and thorough documentation.

Qualifications:

  • Expert in SQL and SAS
  • Deep understanding of database concepts
  • Experienced in Python and packages such as Numpy and Pandas
  • Experience using Jupiter notebooks
  • Solid background in statistics
  • Self-motivated and results oriented
  • Ability to work with vague requirements
  • Ability to work in a multi-disciplinary team

What’s in it for you?

  • Become part of a team that thinks progressively and works collaboratively. We care about seeing each other reach full potential;
  • A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock options where applicable;
  • Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities;
  • Ability to make a difference and lasting impact from a local-to-global scale.

About Borealis AI

Borealis AI, a RBC Institute for Research, is a curiosity-driven research centre dedicated to achieving state-of-the-art in machine learning. Established in 2016, and with labs in Toronto, Montreal, Waterloo and Vancouver, we support academic collaborations and partner with world-class research centres in artificial intelligence. With a focus on ethical AI that will help communities thrive, our machine learning scientists perform fundamental and applied research in areas such as reinforcement learning, natural language processing, deep learning, and unsupervised learning to solve ground-breaking problems in diverse fields.

Inclusion and Equal Opportunity Employment

RBC is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. We are pleased to consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, protected veterans status, Aboriginal/Native American status or any other legally-protected factors. Disability-related accommodations during the application process are available upon request.

title: Data Steward I
description:
Company Overview

Tell us your story. Don’t go unnoticed. Explain why you’re a winning candidate. Think “TD” if you crave meaningful work and embrace change like we do. We are a trusted North American leader that cares about people and inspires them to grow and move forward.

Stay current and competitive. Carve out a career for yourself. Grow with us. Here’s our story: jobs.td.com

Department Overview

With more than 4,500 people in 15 offices around the world, TD Securities provides a wide range of capital market products and services to corporate, government and institutional clients who choose us for our knowledge, innovation and experience in the following key areas of finance:

  • Investment and Corporate Banking
  • Capital Markets
  • Interest Rate, Currency and Derivative Products
  • Commodities

Our services include the underwriting and distribution of new debt and equity issues, providing advice on strategic acquisitions and divestitures, and executing daily trading and investment needs.

With our history of delivering results, we’ve developed considerable strengths, including recognized trading expertise and street-level market intelligence that we use to consistently create value for our clients.

The TD Securities Chief Data Office (TDS-CDO) aims to operate, organize, govern and monetize TDS data assets. This is achieved through four strategic pillars:

  • Make Data Accessible
  • Secure & Govern Data
  • Create Tools & Enable Others to Use Them
  • Derive Value from Data

In doing so, the TDS-CDO enables the dealer with a focus on:

  • Grow: Service clients, monetize data & allocate resources
  • Protect: Manage Risk & Regulatory Compliance
  • Standardize: Achieve technology & operational efficiencies
  • Develop: Data Driven Culture

Job Description

Reporting to the AVP, Data Governance in the Chief Data Office, the Data Steward I will be responsible to support the implementation of data governance and related data management activities for TD Securities.

Job Accountabilities:

  • Support the TDS-CDO in accordance with the Enterprise Data Policy, Standards and Strategy
  • Provides data governance expertise and executes data governance activities
  • Contribute to various operational activities and processes as assigned
  • Be knowledgeable of data management practices and procedures within own area of responsibility and keep abreast of emerging trends for own functional area
  • Assist and help drive the automation of the Data management practices including metadata, data lineage, authoritative sources, data usage, data quality rules, etc.
  • Be knowledgeable of data management tools related to metadata, lineage, data quality, etc. (e.g.: Collibra, Alation, Informatica, Ataccama, etc.)
  • Advises and consults with projects related to implementation of data management practices during system development (data impact analysis, quality controls, data lineage, etc.)
  • Develop and/or prepare applicable business metrics / standard / ad hoc reporting in collaboration with partners and stakeholders for data governance/management area to help inform or support decisions as appropriate
  • Conduct internal and external research projects; support the development / delivery of presentations / communications to management or broader audience
  • Conduct reporting and / or meaningful analysis at the functional or enterprise level using results to draw conclusions, make recommendations, assess the effectiveness of programs/ policies /
  • Support the development of presentations /communications and dissemination of information
  • Coordinate relevant processes /meetings /events that impact the TDS-CDO’s practices
  • Understand department objectives and contribute by recommending appropriate action to management based on analysis and review of results within assigned scope
  • Monitor service, productivity and assess efficiency levels within own function and implement continuous process / performance improvements where opportunities exist
  • Acquire and apply expertise in the discipline, provide guidance, assistance and direction to others
  • Adhere to internal policies/procedures and applicable regulatory guidelines
  • Keep abreast of emerging issues, trends, and evolving regulatory requirements and assess potential impacts
  • Maintain a culture of risk management and control, supported by effective processes in alignment with risk appetite
  • Consistently exercise discretion in managing correspondence, information and all matters of confidentiality; escalate issues where appropriate

Requirements

  • Solid knowledge of data governance/data management practices, enterprise data policy, standards &strategy
  • Solid knowledge of LOB data strategy, data risk management, and data life cycle management
  • Knowledge of current and emerging competitor and market trends
  • Skill in using analytical software tools, data analysis methods and reporting techniques
  • Skill in using computer applications including MS Office
  • Ability to communicate effectively in both oral and written form
  • Ability to work collaboratively and build relationships
  • Ability to work successfully as a member of a team and independently
  • Ability to exercise sound judgement in making decisions
  • Ability to analyze, organize and prioritize work while meeting multiple deadlines
  • Ability to handle confidential information with discretion

Education/Accreditations:

  • Undergraduate degree or technical certificate and/or
  • 5+ years relevant experience

Hours

37.5

Inclusiveness

At TD, we are committed to fostering an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel valued, respected and supported. We are dedicated to building a workforce that reflects the diversity of our customers and communities in which we live and serve. If you require an accommodation for the recruitment/interview process (including alternate formats of materials, or accessible meeting rooms or other accommodation), please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs.

title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer
description:
Now is the time to become an Avidbotter!


About Avidbots

Avidbots is a leading-edge robotics company with the vision to bring robotic solutions into everyday life to increase organizational productivity and to do that better than any other company in the world. Our ground-breaking product, the Neo 2 autonomous floor scrubbing robot, is deployed around the world and trusted by leading facilities and building service companies. Because of our continued successes, we are in major growth mode to add to our award-winning employee culture. Headquartered in Kitchener, ON, Canada, Avidbots offers comprehensive service and support to customers in 5 continents.

About the Opportunity

We are looking for a Senior Machine Learning Engineer to join our Autonomy team. The Autonomy team at Avidbots is responsible for developing production quality software that enables our robots to navigate autonomously in its environment. If you are excited about the world of autonomous robotics and have experience building industrial scale infrastructure for machine learning software, this is a unique opportunity to join a growing team in an emerging product category.

In this role, you will work closely with an experienced engineering team in bringing pioneering machine learning solutions to market for robot perception and navigation applications. You will have direct influence on the design of our end-to-end machine learning infrastructure and data pipelines. The ideal candidate is a self-starter with a proven track record in developing machine learning software in production.

This can be a hybrid opportunity!


How You Will Make a Difference:

● Own deployment of machine learning functionalities into production systems.
● Architect and build scalable software for effective data management, model development, performance validation and metrics monitoring.
● Work closely with other functional teams, QA, Product Manager and Project Manager to integrate machine learning components into our existing robot systems.
● Develop systems for production machine learning model versioning and monitoring.
● Drive continuous improvement to streamline model development and deployment.
● Provide technical leadership and champion software development best practices.

Requirements:

● B.Sc. or graduate degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Machine Learning, Statistics or equivalent
● 5+ years of experience with software development in a professional setting
● 3+ years of experience working with machine learning solutions in production
● Proven experience building scalable infrastructure for machine learning applications
● Experience with industry-standard machine learning frameworks, platforms and tools
● Proficient with programming in C++ and Python
● Ability to troubleshoot machine learning deployment and production issues
● Driven individual, passionate about robotics, self-starter

Bonus:

● Experience with cloud-based machine learning software deployment
● Background in computer vision, perception, or robotics
● Exposure to Robot Operating System (ROS)
● Exposure to Agile project management using industry standard tools such as Jira
● Working knowledge of Linux-based operating systems
● Experience working with a global team

**Applicants must be currently authorized to work in Canada for any employer

Avidbots is an equal opportunity employer committed to building a diverse workforce. As a multicultural organization, we encourage individual achievement and recognize the strength of a diverse workforce. We offer a professional working environment and culture and believe that work / life balance is critical to ensure the happiness/well-being of our people.

Upon request, Avidbots will provide reasonable accommodation for applicants with disabilities throughout the recruitment and selection process.

title: Manager, Data Analysis & Engineering, Global Banking and Markets - Toronto
description:
Requisition ID: 162578

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

Purpose

The Manager, Data Analysis & Engineering role is a part of the Client Insights and Analytics team. The group supports Global Banking and Markets in providing oversight and executing strategic initiatives across all divisions of Global Banking and Markets. This team is responsible for driving the analytics to drive client profitability, sales effectiveness and segmentation. This team is responsible for identifying business opportunities through the use of analytics to drive revenue growth.

This role supports the analysis and strategy functions of Global Banking and Markets using Client MIS data. The incumbent will be heavily involved with data extraction, manipulation, mapping, query & database design, and must be capable of developing new tools and analysis to support the needs of GBM and GBM Executives. This role contributes to the overall success of Global Banking and Markets by providing reporting and insight necessary for evaluation and decision-making purposes.

Is this role right for you?

  • ​​Develop Reporting - Develop meaningful performance and financial reporting; clearly communicate findings.
  • Data Flow Design and Development – Design and create processes that enable reporting and automation.
  • Develop Models and Tools - Build, as required, new analytics, reporting models, and tools.
  • Monthly and Quarterly Reporting - Production, testing, and validation of monthly/quarterly reporting.
  • Perform Analysis - Perform quantitative and qualitative analysis, interpret results.
  • Logic and Algorithm Design - Review, maintain, and develop the algorithms implemented in current tools and processes.
  • Conduct Research - Research and implement best practices for analysis and reporting.
  • Ad Hoc Requests - Support ad hoc requests from Executive Management, auditors, regulators, ratings agencies, research analysts and other external parties as required.
  • Team Environment - Contribute to an environment in which the team works together to produce creative and value-added strategic analyses while ensuring the adequacy, adherence to, and effectiveness of day-to-day business controls.

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role? We'd love to work with you if you have:

  • Undergraduate degree in economics/math/computer science.
  • 2+ years of relevant work experience in capital markets, finance, IT, reporting, or analytics.
  • Strong analytical and investigative skills with proven ability to interpret data, spot patterns and anomalies, and provide insight.
  • Strong creativity: be able to solve difficult problems with innovative or unconventional techniques when standard approaches fail.
  • Excellent oral and written communication and presentation skills.
  • Results-driven and able to grasp and communicate complex ideas clearly.
  • Comfortable working with all levels of management across the organization.
  • Extensive understanding of data and experience with working with multi-million row datasets.
  • Knowledge of algorithm design is a plus.
  • Experienced with Excel (VBA custom functions, Pivot tables, nested formulas, charting), PowerPoint, data mining, and reporting with Tableau/Power BI.
  • Expert knowledge of SQL and Python; have experience developing applications.
  • Strong understanding of and practical experience with modern entity resolution techniques.
  • Self-starter and demonstrated high level of sustained initiative, ability to apply strategic critical thinking to business issues and drive results.

title: Director, Digital Advanced Analytics
description:
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.

At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.

To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com

Job Description

What You’ll Be Doing

Reporting to the Senior Director, Digital Analytics and Integration you will be a leader in the team responsible for implementing Analytics frameworks for Data Science initiatives such as personalization, customer journey analytics, propensity modeling and insights. You will be hands-on analyzing data, working with cross-functional teams, identifying relevant features and building a wide array of models to power and guide strategic initiatives. You’ll work with real world structured and unstructured datasets to produce end-to-end solutions, taking models from experiment to deployment. You’ll interact with groups across the organization from client facing to product, operational, risk and security. You will also lead communicating model recommendations and connecting the outputs to measurable business KPIs and targets.

At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. You’ll have the flexibility to manage your work activities within a hybrid work arrangement where you’ll spend 1-3 days per week on-site, while other days will be remote. Details on your work arrangement (proportion of on-site and remote work) will be discussed at the time of your interview

How You’ll Succeed

Data Science Expertise: Provide advanced analytics solutions to solve business problem leveraging traditional statistical, machine learning and optimization techniques. Ability to conduct self-directed research, typically using non-traditional resources to solve business problems.

Business Consulting: Conduct business problem assessment independently. Gather relevant information related to business processes, functions and operations to evaluate data credibility and quality. Utilize creative thinking to modify or select the most suitable procedure/approach to solve a business problem. Perform in-depth analysis for the purpose of identifying revenue generation and cost saving opportunities, developing proposals and make recommendations for model usage to the downstream partners.

Market Relevancy: Participate in an analytics community whose goal is to foster and share analytic approaches across the enterprise. Liaise with internal and external sources, colleagues, and others to obtain, provide, verify and discuss information and best practices. title: Data Scientist - Financial Crimes - Bilingual
description:
Data Scientist - Financial Crimes - Bilingual

Location: Toronto
Other locations: Anywhere in Country
Date: 30-Oct-2022

At EY, you will have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. And we are counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all.

We live in a new digital world with exciting new technologies and challenges. Businesses are re-evaluating the way they conduct business and undertaking strategic initiatives to enhance their operations to better serve their clients.

At EY, Data Scientist strives to implement innovative approaches to maximize harvests of strategic information, solving many of our clients’ biggest challenges. We are working to integrate this capability into our full range of Consulting service offerings and help clients leverage data as an asset across all their business processes.

The opportunity

Our Canada Financial Crime group is currently seeking a Senior Consultant in Data Science. In the context of anti-money laundering and terrorist financing activities, the group works closely with clients to enhance their ability to use and interpret data and model predictive models. This provides better support for decision-making, and better positioning to meet regulatory obligations. Our attentiveness and closer focus on details give clients an accurate overview and a better understanding of their risks.

The Financial Crime group provides innovative approaches that enable effective, documented and sustainable implementation of projects of high importance to our clients. We are working to integrate this expertise into our Consulting services, as well as all our internal projects, to leverage data as an asset across all business processes.

The Financial Crime group offers expertise and services across four core areas of competency:

  • Data Management and Integration: providing the foundational layers for enterprise data accessibility, covering data sourcing and integration, data warehouse and modeling, master data management, and data rationalisation and migration.
  • Data Quality and Governance: developing an organisation that treats data as a critical enterprise asset and sources quality data for business use and regulatory compliance. This includes examining the fitness for purpose of data for specific business needs, data remediation, and the strategy, roles, policies and processes to effectively manage data through its lifecycle.
  • Reporting design, architecture and implementation of enterprise reporting solutions: This includes the end-to-end reporting lifecycle across advisory and strategy, identifying compliance reporting trouble spots and delivering achievable roadmaps, design and implementation.
  • Analytics: covers operational analytics, predictive analytics, industry models and analytics enablement, and provides actionable enterprise insight and delivers value from an enterprises data.

Your responsibilities

As a Senior consultant in Data Science, you will apply your knowledge and experience to support the delivery of key projects, build valuable relationships both within EY and with clients, and develop strong capabilities through both formal training and working with senior mentors and talented colleagues across different functions. We succeed when we combine high performing team members from a variety of backgrounds. We will provide the required support to transition into the team if this is your first step into consultancy.

Specifically, these responsibilities will be assigned to you:

  • Analyze, manipulate, structure, validate and transform various data sources
  • Produce and document analyses of various data sources
  • Act as a consultant for the implementation of specialized internal data processing solutions
  • Act as a consultant for the configuration of various anti-money laundering and financial crime software
  • Develop filter optimization algorithms to enforce international sanctions
  • Participate in client engagements and various internal projects

Skills & attributes for success

  • Ability to communicate ideas clearly (spoken and written)
  • Ability to provide excellent client service and manage and build strong relationships both internally and externally
  • Ability to be self-sufficient within large companies (financial institutions, specifically)
  • Be versatile, rigorous and curious
  • Enjoy teamwork
  • Have 5-10 years’ experience in data science role

To qualify for the role you must have:

  • Bachelor’s degree in computer science, mathematics, data science, informatics, operations research, engineering or a related field and approximately 3-5 years’ experience in related field. A Master’s degree is an asset.
  • Strong Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and statistics background.
  • Experience with large data sets using tools like SQL, Hadoop, Spark
  • Advanced Programming skills in R or Python, and experience using an object-orientated programming language, such as Java, or Scala (preferred).
  • Experience with model-development lifecycle, data preparation, modeling, and deployment
  • Data processing such as ETL (and ideally some exposure to tools such as Ab Initio, Informatica, etc.), parsing and handling data quality-related issues
  • Demonstrated strong technical problem-solving abilities as well as clear proficiency in handling computer hardware, software, and networks
  • Knowledge of French and English (bilingual)

Ideally, you’ll also have:

  • Consulting experience
  • Experience in other leading ETL packages (e.g. Informatica, DataStage)
  • Experience with cloud technologies (Microsoft Azure, Databricks, AWS, GCP)
  • Experience in Anti-money laundering/terrorist financing activities
  • Experience in Fraud
  • Experience in Risk Management
  • Experience in Business Intelligence
  • Experience in Solution Architecture
  • Experience in Cyber Security
  • Experience in Business analysis/systems

What we look for


We’re interested in passionate leaders with a strong vision and a desire to stay on top of trends in the IT Consulting space. If you have a strong passion for helping businesses achieve their IT strategy, this role is for you.

What we offer

We offer a competitive compensation package where you’ll be rewarded based on your performance and recognized for the value you bring to our business. In addition, our Total Rewards package includes medical and dental coverage, both pension and 401(k) plans, a minimum of 15 days of vacation plus ten observed holidays and three paid personal days, and a range of programs and benefits designed to support your physical, financial and social well-being. Plus, we offer:

  • The opportunity to manage a portfolio of talent within a designated service line
  • A collaborative environment where everyone works together to create a better working world
  • Excellent training and development prospects, both through established programs and on-the-job training
  • An excellent team of senior colleagues, dedicated to managing and varying your workload

Diversity and Inclusion at EY

Diversity and inclusiveness are at the heart of who we are and how we work. We’re committed to fostering an environment where differences are valued, policies and practices are equitable, and our people feel a sense of belonging. From our actions to combat systemic racism and our advocacy for the LGBT+ community to our innovative Neurodiversity Centre of Excellence and Accessibility initiatives, we welcome and embrace the diverse experiences, abilities, backgrounds and perspectives that make our people unique and help guide us. Because when people feel free to be their authentic selves at work, they bring their best and are empowered to build a better working world.

EY | Building a better working world

EY exists to build a better working world, helping to create long-term value for clients, people, and society and build trust in the capital markets.

Enabled by data and technology, diverse EY teams in over 150 countries provide trust through assurance and help clients grow, transform, and operate.

Working across assurance, consulting, law, strategy, tax, and transactions, EY teams ask better questions to find new answers for the complex issues facing our world today.

title: Senior Manager, Data Insight Analytics
description:
Come Work with Us!

At RBC, our culture is deeply supportive and rich in opportunity and reward. You will help our clients thrive and our communities prosper, empowered by a spirit of shared purpose.

Whether you’re helping clients find new opportunities, developing new technology, or providing expert advice to internal partners, you will be doing work that matters in the world, in an environment built on teamwork, service, responsibility, diversity, and integrity.

Job Title

Senior Manager, Data Insight Analytics

Job Description

What is the opportunity?

Our team is the Digital Intelligence Group. We are here to help RBC build best-in-class digital products, optimize client experience, and drive business outcomes through improved use of analytics.

In this role of Senior Manager, Data Insight Analytics, you will provide leadership and support in using analytics, including advanced analytics and statistical models, to generate actionable insights, automate business actions, and improve the speed and quality of decisions across Digital teams at RBC.

You will work with Digital Channels and Products for personal and business clients including Mobile, Online Banking, Direct Investing, NOMI and RBC Rewards. You will structure, curate and complete analyses, develop net new data assets and model data that will enable data-driven decisions.

What do you need to succeed?

Must-have

  • BA/BSc or above in mathematics, economics, statistics, computer science or related quantitative field
  • 7+ years\' experience in related digital product development, marketing analyst or data mining role
  • Advanced experience with Python or Scala, Google Analytics, Tableau, Data Studio and PowerPoint
  • Hands-on knowledge of advanced digital analytics methodologies such as experimentation and testing, competitive analysis, surveys and market research
  • Success with building out an analysis end-to-end with a high degree of independence: i.e., perform lead requirements gathering, develop an analysis plan, wrangle data to suit the problem, apply the right type of analytics and package the output into a presentable deliverable
  • Storytelling and executive presence: ability to articulate a narrative for an analysis or report
  • Experience working on agile teams using Github and JIRA
  • Ability to work with and lead analysts and managers

Nice-to-have

  • MA, MSc or PhD in a quantitative field
  • Strong understanding of AWS Big Data services such as Amazon S3, EMR and Glue
  • Knowledge and experience in areas of data modeling, data orchestration and data pipeline

What will you do?

  • Insights and Analytics:
    • Help advance a culture of timely data-driven decisions
    • Implement practical applications of analytical and statistical frameworks and methodologies to client and prospect data sets to study demographics, behaviors, and business outcomes
    • Use a variety of analytical methods (descriptive, diagnostic and predictive) such as time series analysis, segmentation, hypothesis testing and causal inference to surface actionable insights
    • Embed reliable and re-usable patterns and showcase them to drive forward the analytics brand of the Digital Intelligence Group both internally and across Digital teams at RBC

    • Collaborate with data developers, DevOps, data engineers and cloud engineers to implement new data collection/ingestion/acquisition
    • Support business stakeholders in identifying needs for additional data acquisitions, and coordinate plans to orchestrate data acquisitions

  • Digital Product Reporting and Presentations:
    • Define, communicate and enforce reporting and analytics standards, policies and data governance
    • Construct presentations in story format and deliver them to partners across the organization
    • Deliver clear, concise and timely reports identifying trends, issues and opportunities
  • Stakeholder Consultancy:
    • Manage relationships with key business stakeholders to develop, execute and monitor complex insights and analytical solutions to business problems
    • Digest complex problems and organize into achievable milestones
    • Apply expertise and think creatively to address unique or ambiguous situations and to find solutions to problems that can be complex and non-routine

What’s in it for you?

  • We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.
  • A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock where applicable

title: Development Manager - Applied AI
description:
Development Manager – Applied AI

As an employee at Thomson Reuters, you will play a role in shaping and leading the global knowledge economy. Our technology drives global markets and helps professionals around the world make decisions that matter. Whether solving for today’s challenges or tomorrow’s aspirations, you will work with the industry’s brightest thinkers on diverse projects, creating next-generation solutions that deliver powerful results. As the world’s leading provider of intelligent information, we want your unique perspective to create the solutions that advance our business—and your career.

Are you intrigued by the opportunity to drive the next generation of Legal innovation? If so, we are looking for a Development Manager who will be working on all aspects of software solutions being developed by the Legal Tech Innovation team. The successful candidate will collaborate with team members, other TR development teams, and TR Labs within a dynamic and innovative rapid development environment using the latest Cloud PaaS technologies. The successful applicant will be working with agile teams and stakeholders located locally and in remote locations.

About the Role

In this opportunity as Development Manager you will:

  • Be a People Leader: Enthusiastically manage and motivate a team of software engineers located on-site and remote with varied skills and experience levels. Coach and mentor current and future leaders within the team; be responsible to proactively identify individual training needs, conduct performance evaluations, and maintain a healthy team culture.
  • Be a Project Leader: Strategically plan, design, and implement software to deliver value to customers. Manage multiple projects simultaneously, gathering time and resource estimates. Drive improvements in productivity.
  • Be a Technical Leader: Implement best practices as a knowledge expert in SDLC, object-oriented software design, customer analytics, testing and release, and working in an agile framework. Advocate for quality, performance, scale and agility within the team and with stakeholders. Ensure design and architecture are aligned with the global platform strategy and consider industry trends. Ensure fundamental quality practices are integrated in development lifecycle, such as automated unit tests and integration tests.
  • Be an efficient Stakeholder Manager: Collaborate and engage with external and internal stakeholders such as business teams and product owners to bring products to market. Act as point of contact in resolving stakeholder issues.
  • Be Customer Driven: Deliver high-quality reliable solutions that make S2S APIs easy for our customers to integrate with while continuing to serve their needs.
  • Be a Decision Maker: Utilize problem solving and analytical skills to make quick and effective data driven decisions and roll out strategies that align with company goals.

About You

You’re a fit for the role of Development Manager Technology if you:

  • Possess hands-on experience and deep knowledge in software development and agile development processes.
  • Possess a bachelor\'s degree (or equivalent) in Computer Science, Software Engineering or a related technical field.
  • Have expertise in driving technology strategy and execution, while establishing strategic partnerships and delivering against product requirements.
  • Effectively prioritize work in close alignment with business and IT stakeholders.
  • Possess effective written and oral communication skills with relevant experience leading and influencing others, demonstrating an ability to collaborate effectively with internal and external partners.
  • Deeply understand agile principles and have a proven track record of on-time project delivery following agile development methods and applying best practices.
  • Have experience working in a high-pressure, fast-paced environment with demonstrated multi-tasking skills.
  • Possess the ability to solve problems in large, complex software systems while maintaining a laser focus on system performance and scalability.
  • Have experience and knowledge of JAVA, Python, and AI/ML principles is an asset.
  • Have experience leading cloud based software solutions, preferably AWS.
  • Knowledge of JIRA/ADO, GIT, and Jenkins a plus.

What’s in it For You

At Thomson Reuters, our people are our greatest assets. Here are some of the benefits we offer for your personal and professional growth:

Learning & Development:

  • Exposure to a wide breadth of leading-edge technology
  • Career growth - ability to work on multiple projects and/or with various teams
  • Professional growth and development opportunity through various training programs, conferences, networking events, in-house speaker series etc.
  • Access to Hackathons, Unconferences, Harvard Manage Mentor and more; we offer learning opportunities for everyone

COVID-19 Response:

  • Currently working remotely and following local COVID-19 bylaws and guidelines with most of our offices being remote until July 2021. Upon return, we will be embracing a hybrid work environment consisting of in-office and remote work

Culture:

  • Globally recognized and award-winning reputation for equality, diversity and inclusion, flexibility, work-life balance, and more

Wellbeing:

  • Comprehensive benefit plans; company-wide Mental Health Day Off; Headspace app subscription; retirement, savings, tuition reimbursement, and employee incentive programs; resources for mental, physical, and financial wellbeing

Learning & Development:

  • LinkedIn Learning access; internal Talent Marketplace with opportunities to work on projects cross-company; Ten Thousand Coffees Thomson Reuters café networking, professional growth through various training programs and access to Hackathons

Social Impact:

  • Eight employee-driven Business Resource Groups; two paid volunteer days annually; Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) initiatives for local and global impact

Benefits/Perks:

  • Health benefits, Savings/investment plans and Paid time off (including time off to volunteer and extended parental leave)

Global Opportunities:

  • We have employees in over 90 countries, working across 3 different industries

As a global business we rely on diversity of culture and thought to deliver on our goals. To ensure we can do that, we seek talented, qualified employees in our operations around the world regardless of race, color, sex/gender, including pregnancy, gender identity and expression, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, disability, age, marital status, citizen status, veteran status, or any other protected classification under country or local law. We are proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer providing a drug-free workplace.

#LI-ROBERTTURNERPHR


Do you want to be part of a team helping re-invent the way knowledge professionals work? How about a team that works every day to create a more transparent, just and inclusive future? At Thomson Reuters, we’ve been doing just that for almost 160 years. Our industry-leading products and services include highly specialized information-enabled software and tools for legal, tax, accounting and compliance professionals combined with the world’s most global news services – Reuters. We help these professionals do their jobs better, creating more time for them to focus on the things that matter most: advising, advocating, negotiating, governing and informing.

We are powered by the talents of 25,000 employees across more than 75 countries, where everyone has a chance to contribute and grow professionally in flexible work environments that celebrate diversity and inclusion. title: NLP Application Developer
description:
NLP Application Engineer

We are looking for a NLP Application Engineer to join our growing team to help grow inca, a conversational AI platform for consumer research. Interactive, data-centric, NLP-powered web applications form a key component of our product offering, and you will be working across our stack (Tailwind, React.js, Node.js, Python, AWS) to integrate exciting innovations in AI into delightful web applications, built using best practices and with robust data pipelines. This opportunity will allow you to work in an established startup, and to learn from an experienced team of software developers, market researchers, data analysts, and machine learning researchers.

What You’ll Be Working On:

These are some (but not all) of the ways that you will contribute to our team and product:

  1. Building engaging user interfaces designed for market researchers to accelerate and improve their workflows by leveraging state-of-the-art advances in AI.
  2. Extending cloud-native data pipelines to achieve high-performance, highly-available, and robust processing of semi-structured data at scale..
  3. Improving developer operations and infrastructure including CI/CD, automated testing, system monitoring, security and data protection compliance, etc.
  4. Contributing to product ideation, user experience, NLP experimentation, and application architecture as a core technical member of our team.

Your Skills & Experience:

We look at candidates holistically, and will not rule out any applicant based on a lack of one specific skill set. These are some of the assets that a great candidate would bring:

  • 3+ years of professional full-stack experience in any stack
  • Expertise in some combination of Python, Node.js, or Typescript
  • Familiarity with Git, Docker and other development tools
  • Knowledge of SQL and/or noSQL databases
  • Experience and/or certification in cloud computing (AWS/GCP/Azure)
  • Proficiency in frontend development with intuitive sense of design (React.js preferred)
  • Keen interest in integrating AI capabilities into web applications
  • Real world experience building data-centric applications and/or pipelines


Application:

This position is permanent full-time, and flexibly hybrid (primarily remote, with headquarters in Toronto). If you are interested, please send your CV and a brief email, explaining your interest and describing one or more projects that you’ve worked on, to jobs@nexxt.in.

We particularly encourage applications from groups underrepresented in the technology industry. Check out https://nexxt.in/about to learn more about our team and company culture!

title: Data Analyst - Secondment Position

title: Senior Data Scientist Revenue (Remote, United States)
description:
About the role

Data is a crucial part of Shopify’s mission to make commerce better for everyone. We organize and interpret petabytes of data to provide solutions for our merchants and stakeholders across the organization. From pipelines and schema design to machine learning products and decision support, data science at Shopify is a diverse role with many opportunities to positively impact our success.

Our Data Scientists focus on pushing products and the business forward, with a focus on solving important problems rather than specific tools. We are looking for talented data scientists to help us better understand our merchants and buyers so we can help them on their journey.

Responsibilities:

  • ​​​​​​Proactively identify and champion projects that solve complex problems across multiple domains

  • Partner closely with product, engineering and other business leaders to influence product and program decisions with data

  • Apply specialized skills and fundamental data science methods (e.g. regression, survival analysis, segmentation, experimentation, and machine learning when needed) to inform improvements to our business

  • Design and implement end-to-end data pipelines: work closely with stakeholders to build instrumentation and define dimensional models, tables or schemas that support business processes

  • Build actionable KPIs, production-quality dashboards, informative deep dives, and scalable data products

  • Influence leadership to drive more data-informed decisions

  • Define and advance best practices within data science and product teams

#LI-DNI

Qualifications

  • 3+ years of experience as a Data scientist. Domain experience in e-commerce, marketing or SaaS would be a plus.
  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Information Technology, Mathematics, Computer Science or equivalent is preferred
  • Extensive experience with Python or Pyspark and software engineering fundamentals
  • Deep understanding of advanced SQL techniques, dimensional modeling and scaling ETL pipelines
  • Experience with applied statistics and quantitative modeling (e.g. regression, survival analysis, segmentation, experimentation, and machine learning when needed)
  • Demonstrated ability to translate analytical insights into clear recommendations and effectively communicate them to technical and non-technical stakeholders; Masterful data storytelling and strategic thinking
  • Strong sense of ownership and growth mindset; self-motivated and able to thrive in a remote environment
  • Excellent communication skills

All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

If you’re interested in helping us shape the future of commerce at Shopify, click the “Apply Now” button to submit your application. Please submit a resume and cover letter with your application. Make sure to tell us how you think you can make an impact at Shopify, and what drew you to the role.

As there are multiple positions, this posting will remain live until all positions have been filled. Successful candidates can expect to hear back from us within 2 weeks of application.

#LI-REMOTE

Our belief is that a strong commitment to diversity & inclusion enables us to truly make commerce better for everyone. We encourage applications from Indigenous peoples, racialized people, people with disabilities, people from gender and sexually diverse communities, and/or people with intersectional identities. Please take a look at our Sustainability Reports to learn more about Shopify’s commitments to our communities, and our planet.

At Shopify, we understand that experience comes in many forms. We’re dedicated to adding new perspectives to the team - so if your experience is this close to what we’re looking for, please consider applying.

title: Senior Consultant - Data Analyst
description:
Overview

At KPMG, you’ll join a team of diverse and dedicated problem solvers, connected by a common cause: turning insight into opportunity for clients and communities around the world.

Our Governance, Risk and Compliance Services (GRCS) team is growing and we are looking for a Senior Consultant – Data Analyst to join our team in Toronto.

  • Learn more about Risk Consulting here
  • Learn more about our Toronto office here

What you will do

As a Senior Consultant, you’ll be learning from some of the best in the industry and growing your personal skillset by:

  • Work closely with clients and partners in understanding key business issues on digital opportunities
  • Gather and analyze requirements to develop impactful recommendations and solutions
  • Utilize advanced analytical techniques to solve challenging business problems to identify areas of risk, opportunities and recommend solutions for our clients. Leverage a diverse set of technologies and tools to deliver insights
  • Work in multi-disciplinary and cross-functional teams to develop a strong experience in analytics, statistics, machine learning, data mining, data management, data governance, reporting, visualization and/or intelligent automation
  • Work in a fast-paced and dynamic environment with both virtual and face-to-face interactions; utilize structured approaches to solving problems, managing risks, and documenting assumptions; communicate results and educate others through insightful visualizations, reports, and presentations
  • Work with large volumes of data (structured and unstructured)
  • Proactively contribute to the creation of presentation materials relating to data activities for stakeholder discussions
  • Bridge the gap between technical platform needs and business issues
  • Effectively communicate orally and written with peers within GRCS, KPMG and the client

What you bring to the role

You have strong work experience and academic record and excel in an entrepreneurial environment. You’re highly motivated and passionate professional who strives for excellence, innovation and collaboration, and has the ability to achieve personal and professional success while juggling competing priorities and deadlines.

  • Strong experience of 3-5 years, working with different kinds of messy, real-world data from a variety of databases
  • You’re a highly motivated and passionate professional who strives for excellence, innovation and collaboration, and can achieve personal and professional success while juggling competing priorities and deadlines
  • Independent ability to review the data quality and data definitions and perform data cleansing and data management tasks
  • Strong knowledge and/or experience in the following: SQL, BI tools (like Tableau and PowerBI), Python, R, ETL, Alteryx and RPA tools (like Blue Prism, Automation Anywhere and UIPath)
  • Experience working in a multi-disciplinary team to tackle ambiguous data processing problems across a diverse range of industries
  • Experience in data governance, data quality, data processing, and data controls
  • Client-facing or consulting experience
  • Experience communicating insights or ideas through presentations and meetings
  • Certificate or degree in Computing or IT, with a focus on Business Systems Analysis, Business Intelligence, Data Analysis and Reporting, or similar, is an asset
  • Ability to explain data and analytics concepts to non-technical team members.
  • Ability to apply data and analytics concepts to business problems.
  • Ability to understand and explain business value of data and analytics concepts
  • Strong relationship-building skills to interact with clients, colleagues, and the wider community.
  • Ability to provide crucial consultation, analysis, and recommendations on a broad range of projects in a deadline drive environment with ambiguity.
  • Effectively work as part of a project team, and communicate findings of analysis, key implications, and recommendations in written and verbal formats.
  • Nice to have: Experience in data governance with tools such as Collibra and Informatica

Keys to your success:

KPMG individuals Deliver Impact | Seek Growth | Inspire Trust and understand that a diverse workforce enables us to deepen relationships and strengthen our business.

Providing you with the support you need to be at your best



For more information about KPMG in Canada’s Benefits and well-being, click here.


Our Values, The KPMG Way

Integrity, we do what is right | Excellence, we never stop learning and improving | Courage, we think and act boldly | Together, we respect each other and draw strength from our differences | For Better, we do what matters

KPMG in Canada is a proud equal opportunities employer and we are committed to creating a respectful, inclusive and barrier-free workplace that allows all of our people to reach their full potential. A diverse workforce is key to our success and we believe in bringing your whole self to work. We welcome all qualified candidates to apply and hope you will choose KPMG in Canada as your employer of choice. For more information about Inclusion, Diversity & Equity in Recruitment, please click here.

For general recruitment-related inquiries, please contact the HR Delivery Centre at cafmcdnhrsthotline@kpmg.ca.

If you have a question about accessible employment at KPMG, or to begin a confidential conversation about your individual accessibility or accommodation needs through the recruitment process, we encourage you to contact us at cafmcdnhrsthotline@kpmg.ca or phone: 416-777-8002 or toll free 1-888-466-4778.

title: Project Manager · Toronto, Ontario
description:
POSITION SUMMARY

Reporting to the Director Applied AI Projects, Industry Innovation, the Project Manager will act as an interface between external industry sponsors and internal Vector research activities and ensure the coordination and delivery of Vector industry projects that meet the direct needs of industry sponsors ranging from large enterprises to small startups.

This role is responsible for the ideation and articulation of new projects, as well as all project management functions including monitoring and reporting on project traction and progress, coordinating the development discussion, and approval of corrective action and/or contingency plans.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Engage with industry sponsors to propose AI projects based on specific technical themes, recruit and coordinate project participants, and develop project plans and other required project documentation;
  • Work closely with project participants to define project milestones, success measures, risks and mitigation measures;
  • Develop use cases and pilot projects in collaboration with industry sponsors and facilitate the development of new algorithms and methodologies;
  • Capture project success stories that can be used for public relations;
  • Prepare reporting as required for industry sponsors, Vector’s senior staff & its Board of Directors;
  • Provide support and insight on industry sponsors to the Industry Innovation team; and,
  • Other related duties as assigned.

KEY SUCCESS MEASURES

  • Vector achieves its goals for its industry sponsor relationships.
  • Industry sponsorship growth and retention goals are met.
  • Industry sponsor feedback via informal and formal feedback mechanisms validates that Vector is providing appropriate services and support through effective working relationships.

PROFILE OF THE IDEAL CANDIDATE

  • Holds, at minimum, a university degree in a business or technical field such as computer science, computer engineering or related field; PMP or PMI certification preferred.
  • Experience leading medium-large size projects for a period of two or more years, with consulting or financial industry background preferred.
  • Adept at building and effectively managing multidisciplinary project teams, prioritizing conflicting needs, managing limited resources, handling matters expeditiously, and following through on tasks to successful completion.
  • Possesses exceptional verbal and written English communication skills; effective in presenting to a diverse set of stakeholders from the business and technology ecosystem.
  • Brings a positive attitude and energy, integrity, a sense of humour, and outlook to a corporate culture, and are motivated by continuous learning and the desire to make a difference and a real contribution to AI.

HOW TO APPLY

Please address applications (cover letter and resume) to Sameera Ali, Talent Acquisition Specialist, using the link provided. The deadline for submission is August 12, 2022.


At the Vector Institute we are committed to driving excellence and leadership in Canada’s knowledge, creation, and use of AI to foster economic growth and improve the lives of Canadians. We strive for greater inclusion in the programs and culture that we build by welcoming and encouraging applications from all qualified candidates. This includes but is not limited to applicants who are Indigenous, 2SLGBTQIA+, racialized persons/visible minorities, women, and people with disabilities.

If you require an accommodation at any point throughout the recruitment and selection process, please contact hr@vectorinstitute.ai and we will happily work with you to meet your needs.

title: Staff Data Scientist - Merchant Services (Remote, North America)
description:
About the role

Data science is a crucial part of Shopify’s mission to make commerce better for everyone. We’re the only team able to talk to all of our merchants; we represent their voice here at Shopify. not only when we build for them, but when we figure out what to build and how to build it. This principally includes two types of work: building products directly backed by data (e.g. machine learning models), and listening to their voice to figure out what to build and how to build it.

Our Data Scientists focus on pushing products and the business forward, with a focus on solving important problems rather than specific tools. We are looking for talented data scientists to help us better understand our merchants and buyers so we can help them on their journey.

Team Specific Details:

Within Merchant Services, our job is to build superpowers that put small businesses on a level playing field with the largest e-commerce players.

As a Staff Data Scientist, you are expected to play a crucial role pushing forward product development across multiple domains. Staff Data Scientists do this by informing high-level product strategy and execution, building a strong and broad operating picture allowing us to move quickly, and/or improving underlying algorithms, heuristics and rules powering individual products. We expect a very high degree of autonomy and proactivity, as well as the ability to compound the work of multiple data scientists into powerful conclusions.

Responsibilities:

  • Proactively identify and champion projects that solve critical problems for merchants

  • Partner closely with product, engineering and other business leaders to influence product and program decisions with data

  • Own the operating picture and use a strong analytical toolbox to highlight opportunities and guide execution

  • Build actionable KPIs, production-quality dashboards, informative deep dives, and scalable data products

  • Influence leadership to drive more data-informed decisions

  • Define and advance best practices within data science and product teams

Qualifications

  • 7-10 years of commercial experience as a Data Scientist ​​​​​​solving high impact business problems

  • Proficiency in SQL and experience with software engineering fundamentals

  • Experience with applied statistics and general quantitative problem-solving

  • Demonstrated ability to translate analytical insights into clear recommendations and effectively communicate them to technical and non-technical stakeholders

  • Curiosity about the problem domain and an analytical approach

  • Strong sense of ownership and growth mindset

Experience with one or more:

  • Deep understanding of advanced SQL techniques

  • Expertise with statistical techniques and their applications in business

  • Masterful data storytelling and strategic thinking

  • Deep understanding of dimensional modelling and scaling ETL pipelines

  • Experience launching productionized machine learning models at scale

  • Extensive domain experience in e-commerce, marketing or SaaS

All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

If you’re interested in helping us shape the future of commerce at Shopify, click the “Apply Now” button to submit your application. Please submit a resume and cover letter with your application. Make sure to tell us how you think you can make an impact at Shopify, and what drew you to the role.

As there are multiple positions, this posting will remain live until all positions have been filled. Successful candidates can expect to hear back from us within 2 weeks of application.

#LI-REMOTE

Our belief is that a strong commitment to diversity & inclusion enables us to truly make commerce better for everyone. We encourage applications from Indigenous peoples, racialized people, people with disabilities, people from gender and sexually diverse communities, and/or people with intersectional identities.
