Further, we are committed to fostering an environment of inclusivity and accessibility. If you require an accommodation at any point throughout the recruitment and selection process, please contact hr@vectorinstitute.ai and we will happily work with you to meet your needs.

title: Consultant, Business Analytics - Part Time
description:
• We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.

    At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions so you feel empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and feel truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.

    To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com

    Job Description

    What You’ll Be Doing

    The Process and Change Management (P&CM) team is responsible for facilitating the implementation of operational changes that impact Credit Processing Services (CPS). The Consultant is accountable for providing in-depth advice/consultation typically of an operational or tactical nature, to management, business partners, and team members, within an area of specialization. In this position you will provide business identification and consultative support relative to the development, analysis and identification of process changes and/or system enhancements. You will also proactively analyze and recommend enhancements to the existing business processes; clearly communicate rationale and negotiate solutions which will enhance operational effectiveness. Ensure that all assigned initiatives are implemented in an effective manner within area of responsibility. May lead projects related to the development and implementation of new and revised policies, procedures and standards. Provide technical guidance and professional advice to more junior team members.

    How You’ll Succeed

    • Analytical and Process Improvement Mindset- You have a background in understanding operational processes. You also have a continuous improvement mindset and proven record of adding value by finding efficiencies in current processes and procedures, consequently enhancing the performance of operations teams by actively/proactively participating in project activities.
    • Business Analysis and Operations - You have experience with requirements gathering, designing or modifying processes, managing the change and implementation as well as working with a wide variety of business partners. You will be part of organizing and leading the implementation of operational changes. To be successful, it may be of value if you have an operational processing background to have a good understanding of alternate channel processes, systems and applications.
    • Relationship Builder with Strong Communication Skills - You are proud of your excellent verbal and written communication skills and how you excel at developing relationships with internal and external business partners. You are not afraid to develop and recommend client focused solutions to routine and non-routine operational problems. You welcome the opportunity to represent your area of specialization and provide input into the development and implementation of projects.

    Who You Are

    • Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and you live our values – trust, teamwork and accountability.

title: Senior Director of Engineering, Data & ML
description:
BenchSci's vision is to help scientists bring novel medicine to patients 50% faster by 2025. We do this by empowering scientists to run more successful experiments with the world's most advanced, easy to use biomedical artificial intelligence software platform, thereby avoiding delays that slow the progress of medicine to clinical trials. Backed by F-Prime, Inovia, Golden Ventures, and Google's AI fund, Gradient Ventures, we provide an indispensable tool for more than 41,000 scientists that accelerates research at 15 top 20 pharmaceutical companies and over 4,300 leading academic centers. We're a CIX Top 10 Growth company, certified Great Place to Work®, and top-ranked company on Glassdoor.

We're looking for a Senior Director of Engineering to lead our ML and Data teams. Reporting to the SVP of Engineering, you will work closely with our Engineering and Product leaders to build innovative products, create technical product plans, and bring extensive leadership to help BenchSci scale through our next growth phase.
This position is open to candidates who would like to work remotely (outside of the GTA) or in-person at our Toronto office (post-COVID). For candidates who will be working within the GTA once we return to the office, we will continue to have ongoing remote flexibility of up to 4 days per week (department dependent).

You will:

    • Develop and drive our data & ML roadmap across our products
    • Lead world-class teams of directors, managers, and engineers to support our growth and deliver key data / ML products and capabilities
    • Guide and foster growth by overseeing the organizational design structure for your teams
    • Have an in-depth understanding of the capabilities of our engineering teams and how that impacts other areas of the organization
    • Play an active role in supporting and improving our hiring practices, ensuring that we’re best positioned for our ambitious growth objectives
    • Work with other members of the engineering management team to agree on core values and philosophies towards leadership
    • Actively practice and advocate for our BenchSci Leadership Principles
    • Employ your expertise in order to inspire innovation and adoption of the latest engineering practices
    • Partner in a leading role with Engineering leadership during quarterly and yearly planning meetings

You have:

    • 8+ years of combined experience in machine learning and data engineering
    • 5+ years of experience in a leadership/management role
    • Previous experience shipping software products that have had >50 people working on them
    • Previous experience managing teams of 30+ employees
    • Previous experience in a fast-paced hypergrowth startup
    • Exemplary written and verbal communication skills
    • Experience with distributed systems in production use cases

Nice to haves, but not mandatory qualifications:

    • Previous work experience or educational background in a life sciences-related discipline

Our benefits and perks:

    • A compensation package that includes equity options in the company
    • An annual Executive Health Assessment at Medcan for proactive health awareness
    • An executive coach for managers to assist in leading high-performing teams
    • Complimentary Headspace account to support mental wellness and focus
    • Complimentary genome sequencing from 23andMe to better understand your health through your DNA
    • Three weeks of vacation plus an additional day for every completed year
    • Additional time-off: Winter Holiday (Dec 25-Jan 1), Summer Holiday, your birthday, and more!
    • Generous parental leave benefits with 75% top-up for 20 weeks or Paid Time Off options
    • Work from anywhere flexibility: Every day right now, and up to 4 days per week once we return to the office
    • An onsite gym to help you conveniently stay active and support your physical health
    • A great benefits package that includes health and dental, with an emphasis on mental health coverage
Our Culture:
We believe culture is critical to success and invest accordingly. We live and promote our FASTT values of focused, advancement with speed, tenacity, and transparency. We work hard to maintain an engaging, supportive environment where everyone can do their best work. To learn more, read our culture deck.

Diversity, Equity and Inclusion:
We're committed to creating an inclusive environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive. We believe that improving diversity, equity and inclusion is our collective responsibility, and this belief guides our DEI journey. To learn more, read about our DEI initiatives.

Accessibility Accommodations:
BenchSci provides accessibility accommodations during the recruitment process. Should you require any accommodation, we will work with you to meet your needs.

title: Applied Research Scientist  NLP/ML
description:
Job Description

Are you excited about working at the forefront of applied research in an industry setting? Thomson Reuters Labs in Toronto and Minneapolis is seeking scientists with a passion for solving problems using state-of-the-art Information Retrieval, Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing.

What does Thomson Reuters Labs do? We experiment, we build, we deliver. We support the organization and our customers through applied research and the development of new products and technologies. TR Labs innovates collaboratively across our core segments in Legal, Tax & Accounting, Government, and Reuters News. We undertake a diverse portfolio of projects today while investing in long-term research for the future.

As a scientist in Labs, you will be part of a global interdisciplinary team of experts. We hire specialists across a variety of AI research areas, as well as Engineering and Design, to drive the company's digital transformation. TR Labs is known for consistently delivering projects that advance the state of the art in support of high-growth products and serve Thomson Reuters customers in new and exciting ways.

More about Thomson Reuters Labs – http://tr.com/ai-jobs

About the role

In this opportunity you will:

  • Experiment and Develop: You will execute the entire model development lifecycle, building, testing and delivering high-quality solutions. You will apply best practices to conduct reproducible research and well-managed software delivery.

  • Collaborate: Working on a collaborative cross-functional team, you will share information, value diverse ideas, and partner effectively with colleagues across the globe.

  • Delivery: You will be accountable for timely, well-managed deliverables.

  • Innovate: You will be empowered to try new approaches and learn new technologies.

  • Inspire: You will be a proactive communicator who is excited to share your work. You will be articulate and compelling in describing ideas to both technical and non-technical audiences.

About you

You are a fit for the role if you have:

  • Ph.D. in a relevant discipline or Master's plus a comparable level of experience

  • At least one year of industry experience building IR/NLP/ML systems

  • Solid software engineering skills for prototyping

Preferred qualifications

  • 5+ years academic or industry experience building IR, ML or NLP systems with the demonstrated ability to translate cutting edge research into working applications

  • Proficiency in Python, Java, or Scala and experience delivering minimum viable products in a large enterprise environment

  • Proven communication skills and ability to work on deliverables independently

  • Publications at workshops or conferences such as ACL, EMNLP, NAACL, SIGIR, NeurIPS, or similar

What's in it for you

At Thomson Reuters, our people are our greatest assets. In addition to rewarding and challenging work with exceptional colleagues, we offer the following benefits for your personal and professional growth:

  • Learning & Development: On-the-job coaching & learning, tuition reimbursement, exposure to leading-edge technology

  • Our Scientists and Engineers get to play with big data sets to discover new products, capabilities, and insights for our customers. Thomson Reuters is most well-known for the globally respected Reuters News agency, but our company is also the leading source of information for legal, corporate and tax professionals. We have over 60,000 TBs worth of legal, regulatory, news, and tax data.

  • Benefits: Flexible work arrangements, comprehensive health coverage effective day one, savings and investment plans

  • Perks: Social events & activities, employee discount programs

  • Social Impact: Paid time off for volunteering virtually or in your community

#LI-SJ1

Do you want to be part of a team helping re-invent the way knowledge professionals work? How about a team that works every day to create a more transparent, just and inclusive future? At Thomson Reuters, we’ve been doing just that for almost 160 years. Our industry-leading products and services include highly specialized information-enabled software and tools for legal, tax, accounting and compliance professionals combined with the world’s most global news services – Reuters. We help these professionals do their jobs better, creating more time for them to focus on the things that matter most: advising, advocating, negotiating, governing and informing.

We are powered by the talents of 25,000 employees across more than 75 countries, where everyone has a chance to contribute and grow professionally in flexible work environments that celebrate diversity and inclusion. At a time when objectivity, accuracy, fairness and transparency are under attack, we consider it our duty to pursue them. Sound exciting? Join us and help shape the industries that move society forward.

Accessibility

As a global business, we rely on diversity of culture and thought to deliver on our goals. To ensure we can do that, we seek talented, qualified employees in all our operations around the world regardless of race, color, sex/gender, including pregnancy, gender identity and expression, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, disability, age, marital status, citizen status, veteran status, or any other protected classification under applicable law. Thomson Reuters is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer providing a drug-free workplace.

We also make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and for sincerely held religious beliefs in accordance with applicable law.

description:

title: Deep Learning Engineering Intern, Content & Technology - Fall 2021
description:
We are looking for Deep Learning Interns for our Simulation Technology team! Do you have a passion for computer gaming, virtual reality, computer vision, and artificial intelligence?

Ever dream about inventing your own holodeck? Do you want to work on leading-edge problems alongside some of the best and brightest people in the world? NVIDIA has continuously reinvented itself over two decades. Our invention of the GPU in 1999 sparked the growth of the PC gaming market, redefined modern computer graphics, and revolutionized parallel computing. More recently, GPU deep learning ignited modern AI — the next era of computing. NVIDIA is a “learning machine” company that constantly evolves by adapting to new opportunities that are hard to solve, that only we can pursue, and that matter to the world. This is our life’s work, to amplify human creativity and intelligence.

What you will be doing:

  • You will apply deep learning techniques to the simulation of virtual environments, developing methods to generate synthetic data for training neural networks to achieve results comparable to networks trained on real data

  • Work on improving realism for agents in synthetic environments – using reinforcement learning techniques to control behavior and animation of simulated characters, crowds, and vehicles

  • Conduct research into novel Deep Learning network architectures and techniques for achieving the above.

  • Perform additional development around related technologies as applied to games, VR and visual effects applications.

What we need to see:

  • Currently pursuing a PhD, MS, or BS in Computer Science or related field.

  • 1+ year experience with C++/C, CUDA, DirectX, or OpenGL in addition to Python, PyTorch and TensorFlow.

  • Expertise in neural networks, computer graphics, simulation or game development.

  • Dedication to producing high quality and creative results

Ways to stand out from the crowd:

  • You are a great communicator and self-motivated towards team goals
  • You welcome mentoring others around you
  • Sharp mathematics skills

NVIDIA is widely considered to be one of the technology world’s most desirable employers. We have some of the brightest people in the world working for us and, due to unprecedented growth, our elite engineering teams are rapidly growing. If you\'re a creative and autonomous engineer with a real passion for technology, we want to hear from you.

  • At least 2 years of professional experience; which can include work terms during completion of studies.
  • Strong statistical and analytical skills and interest are an asset.

  • Strong technical knowledge and experience with one or more of: SQL, SAS, MS SQL BI Stack: [SSDS, SSIS, SSRS, SSAS], Tableau, MS Power BI, Python, PySpark, Alteryx, Bitbucket

title: Senior Manager, Client Analytics and Insights
description:
What is the opportunity?

The Client Experience Design & Insights (CXDI) Team consists of COEs from Design Thinking, Market Research, Profitability and Analytics with a focus on the delivery of optimized end-to-end client experiences that are aligned to the collective needs of our clients, employees, and business partners. We steward the Client Experience (CX) service promise of “One RBC” through the creation and application of common CX design principles & standards and unify the organization around holistic client insights.

In the role of the Senior Manager, Client Analytics and Insights you will collaborate with a team of highly specialized subject matter experts to unlock the value of enterprise data to surface deep client and employee insights enabling more targeted strategic response that accelerates business growth as well as creates differentiate value for clients.

What will you do?

  • Client Lifecycle Analytics: Identify actionable insights to enable business partners to make data-driven decision for business strategies and tactics including: Client Acquisition & Onboarding; Activation & Cross Sell; Re-activation & Retention
  • Share of Wallet Analytics: Leverage enterprise data assets to surface insights to better understand client’s RBC and competitor relationship to action product/balance consolidation opportunities
  • Engagement Analytics: Development of advanced insights products to personalize client experience and optimize engagement and loyalty
  • Client Experience Analytics: Identify the relationship between Client Experience / Customer Satisfaction and business results to demonstrate the importance and impact of consistently delivering exceptional client experience
  • Employee Role and Cross Channel Analytics: Surface role and channel behavioral insights to inform distribution strategy and channel investment
  • Segment / Persona Analytics: Creation of client segments and personas as well as client insights on key and emerging segments to inform strategy development and marketing personalization
  • Automation / Process Improvement: Design and improve processes to reduce the manual effort involved in the production of analytics and reports. Seek out opportunities to build re-usable processes (canned code / macros, Tableau templates, etc.) for repetitive tasks to enable efficient delivery of insights.
  • Data Asset Creation: Assembly and manipulate data across multiple data environments such as Hadoop and Teradata using complex queries. Develop, test and deploy data assets that will be leveraged by the Client Insight team as well as other analytical teams to increase speed to insights and consistency of definitions.

What do you need to succeed?

Must-have:

  • A strategic business analytics professional with 5+ years of experience in client analytics and insights
  • 5+ years of experience with relational databases (Oracle, Db2, MySQL, Teradata) as well as 3+ years of experience with big data environment/processing tools such as Hadoop (Hive, HDFS, Pig) and Apache Spark
  • Strong coding experience in programming languages like SQL, Python
  • Strong proficiency with statistical software – SAS
  • Strong experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau
  • Proficiency with statistics and multivariate analytical techniques such as linear/logistic regression analysis to identifying drivers/root cause, composite score development and discriminant analysis
  • Effective storytelling skills – written and verbal. Produce simple, concise presentations and visualizations to clearly communicate to senior leadership. Data translation: Insights- Business Implications - Business Recommendations.
  • Strong consulting mindset to thoroughly understand what he/she is solving for and how their business partners will leverage the insights
  • A ‘Growth Mindset’ – remain current with emerging consumer/industry trends, embraces new data mining tools and different analytical techniques to generate impactful insights.
  • Bachelors in Mathematics / Statistics, Economics, or Computer Science. A Master’s degree is considered an asset

Nice to Have:

  • Experience working in the Financial Services industry
  • Experience with unstructured data such as Google Analytics
  • Experience with Java, Scala

What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.

  • A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation
  • Ability to make a difference and lasting impact
  • Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
  • Opportunities to take on progressively greater accountabilities.

Stay in-the-know about great career opportunities at RBC. Sign up and get customized info on our latest jobs, career tips and Recruitment events that matter to you.

Expand your limits and create a new future together at RBC. Find out how we use our passion and drive to enhance the well-being of our clients and communities at jobs.rbc.com.

title: Instructor – Data Driven Supply Chain & AI Possibilities
description:
This Instructor position is for workshop (lecture and hands-on case study) development and delivery across two half-days, to members of Supply Chain Canada on the use of data analytics.

Dates

This workshop is to be held 1pm - 4pm on December 7 & 8, 2021.

Fees

This is a paid position. The flat fee for this course is $3,000 (CAD) in total for development and delivery of content.

Application

To apply, please submit your CV and a short motivational letter to learn@vectorinstitute.ai

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until filled

Background

This workshop is being created to respond to Supply Chain Canada’s (SCC) interest in learning more about a custom workshop offering on artificial intelligence (AI) from the Vector Institute (Vector). With the seemingly endless applications of AI to the sector, SCC’s constituents wish to learn how to leverage AI to unlock untapped opportunities that add value across the supply chain.

The use of machine learning and AI in supply chain are made possible by the explosion in data from smart transportation, warehouse sensors, barcode systems, and automation across entire value chains. These techniques are used to optimize production, prediction, procurement, personalization, and promotion.

This workshop is designed for non-technical Supply Chain professionals who are well versed in maximizing, minimizing and optimizing various key metrics and are looking to do so with the latest technology and methods. The intended audience include mid-level managers across different supply chain functions, such as procurement, buying, sourcing, warehousing, and distribution.

The workshop introduces technical concepts (e.g., Regression Analysis, Model Optimization) in language that is accessible, using functional examples relevant to the workplace. It is delivered in a lecture/discussion format, and machine learning examples are demonstrated in Python, a popular programming language for AI. Learners are taught to interpret model results and key scoring criteria. The Python scripts are available after the session for those interested in a more hands-on experience.

The instructor, in communication with Vector Professional Development staff, will develop and deliver content in line with the following:

CONTENT: DAY ONE

Introduction: Why does AI matter to supply chain professionals? 10 minutes

AI foundations and key concepts 1 hour

  • · This section covers key terminology and key concepts in AI that supply chain professionals should know.
  • · Introduces technical concepts (e.g., Regression Analysis, Model Optimization) in language that is accessible, using functional examples relevant to the workplace.
  • · Foundations of AI
    • o Key Terminology
  • o What is AI?
  • · Foundations of Machine Learning
    • o The Evolution of AI
  • o Machine Learning
  • o Types of Learning
  • · Neural Networks
  • · AI for Business
  • · Deriving Value from AI
  • · Ethics and Bias

Hands-on exercise 30 minutes Application of concepts

Break 30 Break

AI technologies & supply chain (Section 1) 1 hour

  • Machine learning examples are demonstrated in a popular programming language for AI, Python.
  • Learners are taught to interpret model results and key scoring criteria, and the Python scripts are available after the session for those interested in a more hands-on experience.

CONTENT: DAY TWO

AI technologies & supply chain (Section 2) 1 hour Continuation of Part 1

  • Discussion of hands-on exercise
  • Learners receive a take-home assignment to consider how learnings may be applied in their various work streams (e.g., process improvements)

Hands-on exercise 30 minutes Application of concepts

Break 30

Case Studies in AI for supply chain (1 hour)

  • Introduction to the case studies and discussion.
  • By the end of this workshop, supply chain professionals will be able to identify AI use cases and applications, as well as recognize when to use AI and when to consider other technical solutions.

Proposed case studies:

  • Chatbots/Natural Language Processing (NLP)
  • Predictive Analytics/Demand Forecasting
  • Optimization Modeling


END OF COURSE

  • Assignment review session
  • Course debrief meeting
  • Team wrap meeting


Pedagogical objective

  • To introduce supply chain professionals to key AI concepts and connect them to different supply chain use cases, in order to develop a baseline understanding of how the technologies and methods could add value to their organizations.
  • These professionals may be considering AI-based solutions to improve productivity and efficiency, or they might be part of efforts to evaluate external vendors or make recommendations to leadership on new technology solutions to business problems.

Pedagogical approach (Lecture, remote etc...)

This workshop is delivered in an interactive lecture/discussion format, with practical examples and case studies used to spark reflection and questions. The sessions will be delivered online.

Level of training (introduction, basic competency, specialized competency)

Introduction

Participant prerequisites

  • No prior knowledge of AI required
  • No computer programming experience required

title: Senior Analyst, Patient-Reported Outcome Measures (PROMs) (2)
description:
Senior Analyst, Patient-Reported Outcome Measures (PROMs) (2)

Job number:J0821-1205Job title:Senior Analyst, Patient-Reported Outcome Measures (PROMs) (2)Job type:ContractLocation:Ottawa, Toronto, Ontario, CanadaNumber of positions:2Contract Duration :Until March 2022Date posted:September 14, 2021Closing date:September 28, 2021Range minimum:$77,000.00/year

Who we are

We are an organization comprised of industry thought leaders who are passionate about health data and want to make a difference in the health care field. We are an independent, not-for-profit organization and together with our partners we provide essential information on Canada's health systems, enabling decisions that lead to healthier Canadians. As a valued member of the CIHI team, you and your work will have a pivotal role in the evolution of Canada's health care systems.

CIHI is recognized as an exceptional place to work that embraces diversity, respect, integrity, collaboration and innovation.

At CIHI, we recognize what matters to our employees. Some of the benefits of working at CIHI include

  • HOOPP Pension Plan (Defined Pension)
  • Retirement Planning Program
  • Generous vacation days for permanent and long-term contracts
  • Work-life balance
  • Career Planning Program
  • Learning and Professional Development Program
  • Flexible benefits program from your first day on the job for permanent and long-term contracts

Why is this role important?

Working in a fast-paced environment, the Patient-Reported Outcomes Measurement (PROMs) Senior Analyst is involved in a broad range of activities as a member of an interdisciplinary team. The Senior Analyst is responsible for supporting CIHI’s PROMs programs primarily with a focus on hip and knee arthroplasty. This includes providing research, development and production of comparative measures and reports for health system monitoring purposes. The Senior Analyst will also support the activities of expert groups including research and environmental scans and the preparation of program materials for internal or external audiences.

What you'll do

1. Develops analytical methodologies and measures that support the ability to compare PROMs within Canada and internationally.
2. Responsible for the development and production of materials for PROMs including data analysis, verification, interpretation, writing and reporting. Also includes the preparation and delivery of presentations and promotional products.
3. Conduct and contributes to research and environmental scans for policy relevant analyses. Activities may include formal literature reviews and liaising with internal and external partners.
4. Develops and maintains a detailed knowledge of hip and knee arthroplasty and other condition-specific and generic PROMs.
5. Provides mentoring to other members of the departmental analytical team.
6. Participates in additional projects and activities as required.

What you'll bring to the table

• Preference will be given to candidates with programming experience using SAS and experience in the analysis of large data sets. Experience with R is an asset.

• Proven effective written and verbal communication skills, and excellent interpersonal skills.

• Strong conceptual and problem-solving skills.

• Knowledge of quantitative methods and applied experience in the preparation of information products.

• Proven effective written and verbal communication skills and excellent interpersonal skills; ability to work as part of a multi-disciplinary team in a deadline-driven environment.

• Fluency in English is required, bilingualism is an asset.

Basic Qualifications

• Undergraduate degree in health sciences, health policy, health economics, social sciences, statistics or similar field. A graduate degree would be an asset.

• Three to five years’ experience working in a health care research or policy environment involved in the analysis of research studies and related information products.

To find out more about this role and other exciting opportunities visit our website at www.cihi.ca and check out our 'Careers' section.

We thank all those who apply, however, only candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.

At CIHI we are committed to fostering an inclusive, barrier-free and accessible environment. Part of this commitment includes arranging accommodations to ensure an equitable opportunity to participate in the recruitment and selection process. title: Director, Enterprise Data Layer DevOps
description:
We are the TELUS Data Strategy & Enablement team, our mission is to drive TELUS to become a world-class data insights driven organization, delivering world-class data analytics capabilities built upon unified scalable platforms, advanced AI tooling, high quality data and a data-product/data platform oriented culture. Our team members are passionate, dynamic, entrepreneurial and highly engaged, driven to achieve great outcomes for our customers, our team members, our communities and our environment. We believe in continuous meaningful transformation integrating dynamic change in our professional lives. Our team members are always learning, experimenting and applying tuition value into everything we do.

Join our team
We are searching for a talented candidate to become our team’s Director - Enterprise Data Layer DevOps. The Director will be responsible for the Development and Operations of enterprise foundational platforms providing curated data and insights leveraged across TELUS. The Director will be an agent of change in a very dynamic and fast changing environment and will be the custodian of its evolving strategy, roadmap, execution and maintaining cutting edge cloud skillset. Reporting to the VP – Data Strategy & Enablement, the successful candidate will lead a group of talented data and software engineers and data technology architects working alongside various business units across TELUS to enable the acceleration of insights to actions to savings/revenue generation through efficient creation, distribution & exchange of high quality datasets across TELUS.

This is Your Opportunity
At TELUS, we put ‘Customers First’. With a broad set of responsibilities and a high degree of visibility across the TELUS organization, the Director - Enterprise Data Layer DevOps will have the opportunity to:

  • Lead a team of talented data and software engineers to drive the strategy, plans and execution of our enterprise data and analytics layer in GCP
  • Lead the development and operations of our NiFi and Kafka clusters transporting real time and batch datasets from on-premises to GCP systems
  • Evolve our common ingestion framework (pipeline engineering) processes reducing cost and time to completion while further increasing reliability
  • Manage the evolution and operations of key enterprise visualization systems and data wrangling tools
  • Advance our systems reliability by applying SRE principles optimizing for expected uptime, development of new features and cost
  • Implement and evolve ML OPS principles and tooling to ensure continuous delivery and automation of data pipelines for machine learning
  • Drive the continuous systems transformation as we migrate infrastructure and applications to the cloud

title: TA (Development) Data Driven Supply Chain & AI Possibilities
description:
This TA position is for the development of case studies, a homework assignment and capstone project for members of Supply Chain Canada on the use of data analytics.

Dates: December 7 -8, 2021

Location: This 2-day workshop will be delivered online via Zoom

Instructor: TBD

Overview

This two-day workshop is being created to respond to Supply Chain Canada’s (SCC) interest in learning more about a custom workshop offering on artificial intelligence (AI) from the Vector Institute (Vector). With the seemingly endless applications of AI to the sector, SCC’s constituents wish to learn how to leverage AI to unlock untapped opportunities that add value across the supply chain.

The use of machine learning and AI in supply chain are made possible by the explosion in data from smart transportation, warehouse sensors, barcode systems, and automation across entire value chains. These techniques are used to optimize production, prediction, procurement, personalization, and promotion.

This workshop is designed for non-technical Supply Chain professionals who are well versed in maximizing, minimizing and optimizing various key metrics and are looking to do so with the latest technology and methods. The intended audience include mid-level managers across different supply chain functions, such as procurement, buying, sourcing, warehousing, and distribution.

The workshop introduces technical concepts (e.g., Regression Analysis, Model Optimization) in language that is accessible, using functional examples relevant to the workplace. It is delivered in a lecture/discussion format, and machine learning examples are demonstrated in Python, a popular programming language for AI. Learners are taught to interpret model results and key scoring criteria. The Python scripts are available after the session for those interested in a more hands-on experience.

Tasks and Responsibilities

  • Prepare (typically) homework assignment and resources with direction from the course Instructor(s) in advance of the workshop start date
  • Prepare workshop content for (typically 2, independent) 3h sessions with direction from the course instructor(s) in advance of the course start date
  • Participate in workshop development meetings and engage with workshop development discussions on Slack
  • Curate recommended readings or supplementary materials to support the assignment
  • Ensure that all course materials are submitted (including workshop slides, notes, syllabus, code, scripts, videos, etc.)

Fees

The flat free for this workshop is $1,500 (CAD)

  • All fees are payable at the end of the course ~08 December 2021.
  • TAs must submit invoices at the end of the course

Course Outline

The TA Development, in communication with Vector Professional Development staff, will develop content in line with the following:

CONTENT: DAY ONE

Introduction: Why does AI matter to supply chain professionals? 10 minutes

AI foundations and key concepts 1 hour

  • · This section covers key terminology and key concepts in AI that supply chain professionals should know.
  • · Introduces technical concepts (e.g., Regression Analysis, Model Optimization) in language that is accessible, using functional examples relevant to the workplace.
  • · Foundations of AI
    • o Key Terminology
  • o What is AI?
  • · Foundations of Machine Learning
    • o The Evolution of AI
  • o Machine Learning
  • o Types of Learning
  • · Neural Networks
  • · AI for Business
  • · Deriving Value from AI
  • · Ethics and Bias

Hands-on exercise 30 minutes Application of concepts

Break 30 Break

AI technologies & supply chain (Section 1) 1 hour

  • Machine learning examples are demonstrated in a popular programming language for AI, Python.
  • Learners are taught to interpret model results and key scoring criteria, and the Python scripts are available after the session for those interested in a more hands-on experience.

CONTENT: DAY TWO

AI technologies & supply chain (Section 2) 1 hour Continuation of Part 1

  • Discussion of hands-on exercise
  • Learners receive a take-home assignment to consider how learnings may be applied in their various work streams (e.g., process improvements)

Hands-on exercise 30 minutes Application of concepts

Break 30

Case Studies in AI for supply chain (1 hour)

  • Introduction to the case studies and discussion.
  • By the end of this workshop, supply chain professionals will be able to identify AI use cases and applications, as well as recognize when to use AI and when to consider other technical solutions.

Proposed case studies:

  • Chatbots/Natural Language Processing (NLP)
  • Predictive Analytics/Demand Forecasting
  • Optimization Modeling

Pedagogical objective

  • To introduce supply chain professionals to key AI concepts and connect them to different supply chain use cases, in order to develop a baseline understanding of how the technologies and methods could add value to their organizations.
  • These professionals may be considering AI-based solutions to improve productivity and efficiency, or they might be part of efforts to evaluate external vendors or make recommendations to leadership on new technology solutions to business problems.

Pedagogical approach (Lecture, remote etc...)

This workshop is delivered in an interactive lecture/discussion format, with practical examples and case studies used to spark reflection and questions. The sessions will be delivered online.

Level of training (introduction, basic competency, specialized competency)

Introduction

Participant prerequisites

  • No prior knowledge of AI required
  • No computer programming experience required

To apply, please submit your CV and a statement outlining your motivation and objectives to: learn@vectorinstitute.ai

title: Analyst, Application Development & Maintenance - New Capability Distribution - Temporary
description:
Description

Being part of Air Canada is to become part of an iconic Canadian symbol, recently ranked the best Airline in North America. Let your career take flight by joining our diverse and vibrant team at the leading edge of passenger aviation.

Air Canada is looking for an experienced and dynamic technical Analyst Application Development & Maintenance (Temporary) focused on providing IT support to the Air Canada’s Product distribution branch focusing on the maintenance and development of our New Capability Distribution (NDC) solution. This role requires collaboration with a diverse internal workforce and extended third party service providers.

These are exciting times for IT at Air Canada, with several major projects underway that will transform its IT landscape. This is a unique opportunity to work with state-of-the-art technologies and to contribute to the evolution of the Air Canada’s NDC solution for its partners, a recognition program for corporate customers as well as optimize existing functionality.

This position will be reporting to the IT Manager-responsible for NDC portal and the Air Canada B2B Agent portal.

Responsibilities:

  • Develop, deliver, and maintain the NDC portal and the Air Canada agent portal as per desired technology architecture and business requirements while ensuring highest quality of end-products through introduction of new tools, technology, and practices for evolution of processes & optimization of applications.
  • Troubleshoot partner issues with IT cross functional teams and provide solutions promptly.
  • Work closely with relevant stakeholders to collect and use information for improved operational and commercial performance.
  • Collaborate with vendors and internal users to develop, evaluate, install, and test software enhancements.
  • Review and analyze data from multiple internal and external stakeholders.
  • Maintain and update technical documents, flowcharts, and procedures.
  • Communicate analysis results and making recommendations to relevant stakeholders.
  • Provide support for the business analysis and technology/business integration efforts.
  • Conduct requirement and systems analysis and translate business requirements.
  • Support multiple working methodologies such as Hybrid, Agile, DevOps.
  • Support planning and monitoring processes.
  • Provide management with economic impact and compliance issues surrounding key business decisions support definition and maintenance of methods, techniques, and calculations for identifying ways to improve business/technical processes.

Qualifications

  • At-least 3 years of IT technology or operations experience in a large company preferably in the Transportation/Airline domain would be an asset.
  • A relevant University degree/technical certification, and/or relevant experience commensurate to the role.
  • Experience working with NDC (New Distribution Capability) APIs and schemas in an airline distribution environment.
  • Familiarity with airline offer and order management standards as defined by IATA NDC implementations.
  • Understanding of ITIL, SDLC, Agile methodologies, Jira, and cyber security risk management frameworks are assets.
  • Knowledge of APIs and Tools like SOAP UI/Postman is a must. Knowledge of Mulesoft will be an added advantage.
  • Exceptional analytical, organizational and communication skills.
  • Self-motivated and independent worker.
  • Possess investigative nature and be self-motivated.
  • Results oriented with proactive and methodical approach to problem solving.
  • Able to multi-task and work under pressure against tight deadlines and changing priorities.
  • Must be a team player with ability to work closely with diverse groups and working styles.
  • Ability to establish and maintain effective business relationships.
  • Good knowledge of implemented web/ application initiatives.
  • Ability to following processes and directives.
  • Knowledge with User Acceptance Testing, Quality assurance testing, and has impact assessments skills.
  • Flexibility and willingness to work extended hours, when required.
  • Demonstrate punctuality and dependability to support overall team success in a fast-paced environment.

Conditions of Employment:

  • Candidates must be eligible to work in the country of interest, at the time any offer of employment is made and seeking any required work permits/visas or other authorizations which may be required is the sole responsibility of the candidates applying for this position.

Linguistic Requirements

Based on equal qualifications, preference will be given to bilingual candidates.

Diversity and Inclusion

Air Canada is strongly committed to Diversity and Inclusion and aims to create a healthy, accessible and rewarding work environment which highlights employees’ unique contributions to our company’s success.

As an equal opportunity employer, we welcome applications from all to help us build a diverse workforce which reflects the diversity of our customers, and communities, in which we live and serve.

Air Canada thanks all candidates for their interest; however only those selected to continue in the process will be contacted.

description:
​Req Id: 425839

At Bell, our purpose is to advance how Canadians connect with each other and the world. We achieve this by providing consumers and businesses with the best network technologies in the world, innovative digital solutions and seamless customer experiences.   • Solving complex challenges with creative and innovative solutions.
  • Developing B2B solutions and shaping new product roadmaps.
  • Working with the latest technologies in a collaborative and supportive environment.
  • Making impactful decisions and driving projects that contribute to Bell's strategic goals.
  • Gain breadth of experience to help transform the organization and become the next generation of leaders at Bell!

What we’re looking for

Requirements

  • You have a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Software Development, Computer Science, Information Technology or related field.

Technical Skills (we're looking for individuals with):

  • You have knowledge of, and the capability and passion to continuously learn, different programming languages (ex. Java, JavaScript, Python, C#. NET, GOLANG)
  • You are familiar with SAP programming (ABAP). You are interested in web development (IE, ReactJS, Angular, VueJS)
  • You are familiar with different working methodologies i. e. DevOps, CI/CD, TDD/BDD
  • You have a desire to learn how to develop and work with cloud technologies (AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes, Docker, Openshift (OCP) etc.)
  • You have an interest in data management (SQL and Non-SQL databases: PostgreSQL, MariaDB, MongoDB, Redis, Cassandra, Oracle, SQL Server) and caching tools (Redis, Red Hat DataGrid, SOSS)
  • You are open to explore different avenues of software development such as (but not limited to) front-end, back-end or full stack development Willing to learn

Leadership Skills

  • You are a skilled communicator balancing urgency, empathy and integrity. You speak and write clearly and concisely, and you listen with the purpose of encouraging transparent dialogue.
  • You gain the trust of your teams, being known for demonstrating fairness and respect.
  • You promote belonging and are flexible, accountable and collaborative.
  • You are analytical and are an exceptional problem-solver with a curiosity to understand "how things work”.
  • You can identify insights and opportunities from data and metrics and are able to create strategies to support sound recommendations.
  • You can simplify complex issues, make good and timely decisions, and be an accountable member of a team.
  • You are willing to challenge the status quo, experimenting to find solutions that will make a difference in the lives of Bell’s customers.
  • You are goal-oriented and can focus on individual tasks while also understanding the bigger picture and how systems work together.
  • You are committed to growing your skills and learning from others.
  • Your leadership qualities have been demonstrated through extracurricular activities and projects.

Applying is simple

Our application process is designed to be straightforward, so you can show us who you are and we can easily spot your #TeamBell potential.

  1. Review our programs and indicate up to three role choices in your application.
  2. Complete your video interview within 72 hours.
  3. If selected, you will be invited to complete a phone interview or assessment to better understand your skills and experience.
  4. If selected from previous step, you will be invited to a virtual panel interview.

Get started by clicking Apply on this page. Submit a resume, and most recent transcripts (unofficial accepted) to be considered.

title: Senior Manager, Data Scientist
description:
Job Description

What is the opportunity?

The Global Economic Sanctions (GES) team is a unique function within the Financial Crimes that provides support to Global Financial Crimes teams and Business Units at an enterprise level for Canadian, US, UK, EU/Europe, APAC and Caribbean economic sanctions.

We are seeking an experienced and technically skilled Senior Manager to lead the design, execution, and oversight of data-driven processes supporting our Sanctions Risk Assessment framework. This role will own the end-to-end automation, aggregation, calculation and data quality check process, ensuring alignment with policy requirements and risk methodologies, while managing complex stakeholder interactions across risk, technology, and business units.

This role combines technical skills, risk acumen, process oversight, and strong interpersonal skills to ensure the end-to-end execution of the risk assessment data-driven process is robust, efficient, and aligned to enterprise expectations.

What will you do?

Data Requirements & Analysis

  • Translate sanctions risk methodology into structured data pull and processing requirements.

  • Assess data availability across assessment units to drive accurate and tailored data sourcing.

  • Collaborate with data owners and SMEs to define, validate, and refine source data elements and extraction logic.

Client & Product Mapping

  • Contribute and oversee the logic for identifying active clients, including inclusion/exclusion criteria.

  • Conduct review and challenge for mappings between products, assessment units, and clients to ensure consistency and traceability.

Automation of Risk Tools

  • Drive the automation of Control Self-Assessment (CSA), Qualitative Risk Questionnaires (QRQ), and Product & Service Questionnaires (PSQ).

  • Enhance and integrate scripts to streamline execution, reduce manual effort, and improve accuracy.

  • Ensure automation reflects evolving requirements and maintains alignment with policy and methodology changes.

Configuration & Tailoring

  • Maintain and update threshold tables and configuration files, tailoring logic to reflect assessment unit-specific requirements.

  • Ensure changes are fully documented, reviewed, and regression tested.

Aggregation & Calculation Logic

  • Develop and maintain robust scripts to perform quantitative data aggregation, scoring, and risk calculations at scale.

  • Ensure accuracy, performance, and auditability of aggregation and calculation processes.

Data Quality Automation & Outlier Analysis

  • Design and implement automated data quality checks to identify inconsistencies, discrepancies, or misalignments in critical data elements.

  • Highlight anomalies or indicators of elevated risk requiring further investigation and/or local engagement.

  • Review script outputs to verify that logic aligns with requirement documentation and meets defined risk objectives.

  • Conduct detailed analysis and validation to ensure outcomes support the risk methodology.

End-to-End Process Ownership

  • Own and oversee the entire lifecycle of data sourcing, transformation, automation, and output validation for sanctions risk assessment.

  • Document, monitor, and enhance each stage of the process to ensure performance, resilience, and compliance.

Leadership & Stakeholder Management

  • Act as the key point of contact for senior stakeholders in Risk, Compliance, Technology, Data Governance, and the Business.

  • Communicate complex technical issues in a clear, structured way to non-technical audiences.

  • Navigate and lead difficult conversations to resolve disagreements, clarify responsibilities, or address delivery gaps.

  • Prioritize competing demands and manage multiple timelines effectively.

  • Assist in other governance responsibilities, as required, including.

  • Produce sanctions dashboard and management reporting on sanctions compliance program.

  • Assist in audit, testing or regulatory exams related to sanctions.

  • Assist in tracking and closing sanctions-related issues.

  • Assess and review sanctions policies and procedures developed by global business units and/or local compliance teams; review and recommend amendments to the enterprise sanctions policy based on knowledge of the global sanctions environment.

  • Develop and update sanctions related training.

  • Complete other ad-hoc assignments supporting GES program oversight team.

What do you need to succeed?

Must-have

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Risk Management, Statistics, or related field.

  • 6–10+ years of experience in data analysis, process automation, or technology roles, preferably in financial services or compliance.

  • Strong coding skills in Python (e.g. Pandas), with experience automating data pipelines and risk tools.

  • Demonstrated ability to manage end-to-end project lifecycle from design to deployment and monitoring.

  • Excellent writing and analysis skills, particularly in risk assessment script documentation.

  • Proven stakeholder management skills across cross-functional teams and senior leaders.

  • Strong time management and organizational discipline under tight deadlines.

  • Experience leading teams or complex cross-team initiatives with minimal oversight.

Nice-to-have

  • Experience with version control systems (e.g., Git) and data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau).

  • Understanding of RBC data governance principles and data management.

  • Experience with Dataiku.

  • Experience with risk frameworks, controls, or regulatory reporting.

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. title: Data Engineer, Data Integration & Performance Platform
description:
Choose a workplace that empowers your impact.

Join a global workplace where employees thrive. One that embraces diversity of thought, expertise and experience. A place where you can personalize your employee journey to be — and deliver — your best.

We are a purpose-driven, dynamic and sustainable pension plan. An industry leading global investor with teams in Toronto to London, New York, Singapore, Sydney and other major cities across North America and Europe. We embody the values of our 600,000+ members, placing their best interests at the heart of everything we do.

Join us to accelerate your growth & development, prioritize wellness, build connections, and support the communities where we live and work.

Don’t just work anywhere — come build tomorrow together with us.

Know someone at OMERS or Oxford Properties? Great! If you\'re referred, have them submit your name through Workday first. Then, watch for a unique link in your email to apply.

This is your chance to launch your data engineering career with a dynamic team supporting OMERS Capital Markets, Total Portfolio Management, and Corporate Finance. You\'ll work alongside investment professionals to develop and maintain data solutions that power our pension promise. We welcome recent graduates and early-career professionals (<5 years experience) who are eager to grow their skills in financial data systems.

The official title within the team is Technical Analyst, Data Integration & Performance Platform

We believe that time together in the office is important for OMERS and Oxford, the strength of our employees, and the work we do for our pension members. Our flexible hybrid work guideline requires teams to come to the office a minimum of 4 days per week.

What You\'ll Do

  • Data Integration & Engineering:

    • Develop and maintain database objects (tables, stored procedures, functions).

    • Integrate structured and unstructured data into investment data warehouses and BI solutions.

    • Design and deploy data pipelines and infrastructure to support analytics, reporting, and downstream systems.

  • Reporting & Analytics Collaboration:

    • Build and optimize reports/dashboards using SSRS, Power BI, or similar tools.

    • Partner with analysts to translate business needs into technical solutions (e.g., stored procedures, data models).

  • Process Optimization & Automation:

    • Support, automate, and optimize existing data processes (ETL, scheduled jobs, workflows).

    • Troubleshoot data issues and improve system performance.

  • Technical Development & Project Delivery:

    • Maintain web applications (ASP.NET) and integrate data across financial platforms (e.g., CRD, Bloomberg).

    • Define requirements, implement system interfaces, and assist in workflow automation.

    • Implement controls and standards for data quality and application governance.

  • Stakeholder Engagement & Support:

    • Provide user support for applications, reports, and ad-hoc data requests.

    • Collaborate with internal teams (IT, investments) and external vendors to enhance systems.

What We\'re Looking For

Must-Have Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Engineering, or related field.

  • 1-5 years of experience or academic projects involving:

    • SQL and relational databases (e.g., SQL Server, Oracle).

    • Programming (C#, Java, Python, or similar).

    • Data analysis and problem-solving.

  • Strong communication skills to bridge technical and business teams.

  • Ability to juggle priorities in a fast-paced environment.

Nice-to-Have Experience:

  • Exposure to financial systems (CRD, Bloomberg, Calypso, Eagle Pace).

  • Experience with Generative AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Midjourney, or similar).

  • Familiarity with prompt engineering and context optimization for AI-driven workflows.

  • Ability to leverage AI for content generation, automation, or data analysis.

  • Knowledge of LLM fine-tuning, RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), or AI-assisted development is a plus.

  • Scripting (PowerShell, VBScript) or Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps/Automate).

  • Web development (ASP.NET, JavaScript) or cloud data tools (Azure, AWS).

  • Internships or projects in financial data pipelines or BI solutions.

As one of Canada’s largest defined benefit pension plans, our people-first culture is at its best when our workforce reflects the communities where we live and work — and the members we proudly serve.

From hire to retire, we are an equal opportunity employer committed to an inclusive, barrier-free recruitment and selection process that extends all the way through your employee experience. This sense of belonging and connection is cultivated up, down and across our global organization thanks to our vast network of Employee Resource Groups with executive leader sponsorship, our Purpose@Work committee and employee recognition programs.

title: Generative AI Data Scientist
description:
Req Id: 426465

At Bell, our purpose is to advance how Canadians connect with each other and the world. We achieve this by providing consumers and businesses with the best network technologies in the world, innovative digital solutions and seamless customer experiences. It’s all developed and delivered by the members of #TeamBell and we’re always on the lookout for people with great skills and experiences.

We provide a supportive and inclusive community where all team members can succeed. And through our commitment to environmental, social and governance initiatives, you will feel good about the greater impact you will have – making every day better for people as they connect, work, learn and play.

Join us. You belong at Bell.

Summary

Are you passionate about language and harnessing its power with cutting-edge AI? Do you thrive in a dynamic environment where innovation drives the future? If so, we want you!

Key Responsibilities

  • Design, train, and deploy NLP models for various applications within the telecommunications domain. This could include tasks like sentiment analysis, topic modeling, chatbot development, and more.
  • Work collaboratively with engineers, product managers, and data analysts to translate business needs into actionable NLP solutions.
  • Explore and implement cutting-edge Generative AI (GenAI) techniques to create innovative applications for our telecommunications services.
  • Continuously monitor and evaluate the performance of NLP models, identifying areas for improvement and optimization.
  • Participate in research initiatives to stay ahead of the curve in NLP and GenAI advancements.
  • Clearly document your work and present findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.

Critical Qualifications

  • Bachelors or Master's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Linguistics or a related field.
  • Experience with Large Language Models (LLMs) and Generative AI (GenAI) technology, including but not limited to OpenAI API, ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Langchain, and HuggingFace Transformers.
  • Research, design, build, and train innovative applications of LLMs.
  • Proficient in Python programming, with experience utilizing data manipulation libraries such as Pandas, NumPy, and Scikit-learn.
  • Ability to write and optimize complex SQL queries for data analysis and manipulation. Experience working with various SQL databases, including but not limited to MySQL, PostgreSQL, and
  • BigQuery. Understanding of relational database concepts and data modeling is essential.
  • Proven experience in designing, training, and deploying NLP models using frameworks like TensorFlow or PyTorch.
  • Strong understanding of machine learning algorithms and their application in NLP tasks.
  • Experience with version control (Git) and testing methodologies (unit/integration)
  • Experience designing and implementing RESTful APIs for backend applications, with a strong focus on data-centric services
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to diverse audiences.
  • A passion for innovation and a strong desire to push the boundaries of NLP with GenAI.

Preferred Qualification

  • Experience working within the telecommunications industry.
  • Knowledge of cloud computing platforms like GCP, AWS or Azure.
  • Familiarity with GenAI techniques like text generation, summarization, or machine translation.
  • Experience with prompt engineering.

Adequate knowledge of French is required for positions in Quebec.

We know that caring for our team members is at the heart of a healthy, positive and thriving workplace. As part of our team, you’ll enjoy a comprehensive compensation package that includes a competitive salary and a wide range of benefits to support the well-being of you and your family. Plus, as a Bell team member, you'll enjoy a 35% discount on our services and access exclusive offers from our partners.

title: AI Data Trainer, English Language
description:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Why this role?

We are on a mission to build machines that understand the world and make them safely accessible to all. Data quality is foundational to this process. Machines (or Large Language Models to be exact) learn in similar ways to humans - by way of feedback. By labelling, ranking, auditing, and correcting text output, you will improve Large Language Model’s performance for iterations to come, thus having a lasting impact on Cohere’s tech.

Please Note: This is a part-time independent contractor position available within Canada only. We seek candidates who can commit to 16 hours per week at a CAD $30/hour contract rate. This role is BYOD 💻 - Bring Your Own Device (laptop). This position is remote. Performance incentives included!

As an AI Data Trainer, you will:

  • Label and Rank: Accurately label and rank machine learning data, ensuring data integrity and quality.

  • Audit and Correct: Scrutinize and rectify any inaccuracies in language data (may include text, image, JSON, TSV/CSV, Markdown), maintaining the highest standard of data accuracy.

  • Read and Complete Text-Based Tasks: Efficiently complete reading and text-based assignments, with high attention to detail.

  • Perform Preference-Based Tasks: Evaluate and complete tasks, assessing which responses best conform to project guidelines.

  • Recommend Optimizations: Identify and suggest opportunities to improve data quality and model performance in language tasks.

  • Provide Feedback: Collaborate and communicate effectively, providing feedback to cross-functional team members, particularly on language-related matters.

  • Have Detail-Oriented Execution: Maintain meticulous attention to detail while performing repetitive and precise tasks, particularly when working with language data.

You may be a good fit if you have:

  • Language Proficiency: Native or near-native proficiency in English

  • Expert reading and writing skills, which you are ready to prove on our written test

  • Basic familiarity with JSON/TSV/CSV/Markdown data manipulation

  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to accuracy - you’re the type to proofread all of your emails!

  • High tolerance for repetitive and monotonous work

  • Ability to follow complex instructions, navigate ambiguity and work independently

  • Superb sense of urgency and time management

  • Curiosity about technology and a knack for tackling problems in creative ways

  • A reliable laptop and internet connection

The Candidate Journey:
Initial Screening - Once you have submitted your application our Talent Team will review your resume and writing samples.
Multiple Choice Test - This multiple-choice test will provide an initial assessment of your language skills and attention to detail.
Written Language Test - This online test will assess your written skills through various language-based tasks, such as a writing sample or editing task.
Video Interview - If selected to move forward, you will have a short video call with a member of our Operations Team!
Contract - You will receive and sign your Independent Contractor Agreement.

As an independent contractor, you maintain control over how you complete your work and may work with multiple clients simultaneously, although we ask you to declare if any of these are with a direct competitor of Cohere and maintain IP confidentiality of the Cohere project. Independent contractors are not eligible for health benefits or other benefits provided to employees. Compensation for services is provided to contractors by contractors invoicing for services provided pursuant to the terms of our agreement with the contractor.

It is important to understand that as an independent contractor, continuous work is not guaranteed. The client-contractor relationship is fundamentally project-based, meaning engagements may be temporary, periodic, or intermittent based on our organizational needs and project availability. As an independent contractor, you should anticipate fluctuations in workflow and, therefore, compensation for services when Cohere does not require as many hours of services in a week.

Prospective candidates, please be advised: this role involves working with human-generated and model-generated tasks that may involve exposure to not safe for work (NSFW) text content as part of data annotation tasks, including explicit, offensive, or other inappropriate material.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for 6 months for employees based in Canada, the US, and the UK

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation

Note: This post is co-authored by both Cohere humans and Cohere technology.

title: AI Product Manager
description:
Join our team and what we\'ll accomplish together

Join our team to transform TELUS Network with the power of AI

The Chief AI Office (CAIO) team empowers TELUS to become a world-class AI-driven organization. Our AI Accelerator team drives this transformation by partnering with business units to unlock exponential value through data and insights.

As an AI Product Manager, you will bridge the gap between cutting-edge AI capabilities and tangible business impact within the CTO portfolio. You will act as a consultant and change agent, championing AI adoption and ensuring its effective application to revolutionize decision-making.

What you\'ll do

  • Partner with CTO stakeholders to identify and prioritize high-impact opportunities to leverage AI within their business processes and operations
  • Lead workshops and discovery sessions to understand current challenges and translate business needs into well-defined AI solution requirements
  • Collaborate with data scientists and engineers, acting as the voice of the business to shape the development and delivery of AI solutions
  • Develop and execute change management strategies to ensure successful adoption of AI solutions
  • Define key performance indicators (KPIs) and track the impact of AI solutions on business outcomes
  • Continuously assess the evolving AI landscape and identify new opportunities to drive innovation

What you bring

  • Proven track record of delivering data-driven solutions and driving business impact
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and consulting skills, with the ability to understand business needs and translate them into technical requirements
  • Excellent communication, influencing, and facilitation skills to build rapport, manage stakeholders, and explain complex concepts clearly
